part.text:
Let me start by understanding the codebase. This is the mise project (Rust CLI tool for managing dev environments). The task is about GitHub release attestations where each file inside an archive is attested (rather than the archive blob itself). I need to make mise accept those safely, with fail-closed fallback.

Let me explore the codebase to understand the current attestation/verification system.

todos.content: Explore existing attestation/verification code in the codebase
Understand how archive-level verification currently works
Implement the change preserving existing archive-level verification

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: //! Sole mise-internal bridge to the `mise-sigstore` crate.
2: //!
3: //! Every call mise makes into `mise_sigstore` goes through this module. Callers never
4: //! touch the underlying crate directly and never pass a GitHub token — the token is resolved
5: //! internally via [`crate::github::resolve_token_for_api_url`], which walks the full chain
6: //! (env vars → `credential_command` → `github_tokens.toml` → gh CLI → git credential fill).
7: //!
8: //! ## Why this exists
9: //!
10: //! Before this module, three sigstore call sites (`src/backend/aqua.rs`,
11: //! `src/plugins/core/python.rs`, `src/plugins/core/ruby.rs`) passed
12: //! `crate::env::GITHUB_TOKEN.as_deref()` — env vars only — while the github backend used the
13: //! full chain. That asymmetry left `mise lock` issuing unauthenticated attestation requests,
14: //! which hit GitHub's 60/hour IP rate limit after the second run.
15: //!
16: //! Concentrating the `mise_sigstore` surface here makes the asymmetry
17: //! structurally impossible: wrapper signatures omit the token argument, so callers cannot
18: //! re-introduce the bug without first editing this file.
19: //!
20: //! ## Default API URL
21: //!
22: //! Functions that accept `api_url: Option<&str>` fall back to [`crate::github::API_URL`]
23: //! (`"https://api.github.com"`) when `None` is passed. The default must be a full URL so
24: //! [`crate::github::resolve_token_for_api_url`] can parse the host correctly; a bare hostname
25: //! would be silently misrouted for GitHub Enterprise Server tenants.
26: 
27: use std::path::Path;
28: 
29: use mise_sigstore::sources::github::GitHubSource;
30: use mise_sigstore::{ArtifactRef, AttestationSource};
31: 
32: pub use mise_sigstore::AttestationError;
33: 
34: /// Result alias that matches `mise_sigstore`'s internal convention.
35: type AttestationResult<T> = std::result::Result<T, AttestationError>;
36: 
37: /// Resolve a GitHub token for an optional API base URL, defaulting to [`crate::github::API_URL`].
38: fn resolve_token_for_wrapper(api_url: Option<&str>) -> Option<String> {
39:     let url = api_url.unwrap_or(crate::github::API_URL);
40:     crate::github::resolve_token_for_api_url(url)
41: }
42: 
43: /// Verify a GitHub artifact attestation for a file on disk.
44: ///
45: /// Dispatches to [`mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation_with_base_url`] when
46: /// `api_url` is `Some` (to support GitHub Enterprise) and to
47: /// [`mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation`] otherwise.
48: pub async fn verify_attestation(
49:     artifact_path: &Path,
50:     owner: &str,
51:     repo: &str,
52:     expected_workflow: Option<&str>,
53:     api_url: Option<&str>,
54: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
55:     let token = resolve_token_for_wrapper(api_url);
56:     match api_url {
57:         Some(base_url) => {
58:             mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation_with_base_url(
59:                 artifact_path,
60:                 owner,
61:                 repo,
62:                 token.as_deref(),
63:                 expected_workflow,
64:                 base_url,
65:             )
66:             .await
67:         }
68:         None => {
69:             mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation(
70:                 artifact_path,
71:                 owner,
72:                 repo,
73:                 token.as_deref(),
74:                 expected_workflow,
75:             )
76:             .await
77:         }
78:     }
79: }
80: 
81: /// Reason the pre-download attestation probe could not complete.
82: ///
83: /// Preserved as two variants so callers can log distinct warnings for a misconfigured
84: /// endpoint (source creation) versus an API/network error (fetch). The original inline
85: /// pre-wrapper code at `src/backend/github.rs` emitted different messages for each; the
86: /// wrapper keeps that signal instead of flattening both into one error string.
87: #[derive(Debug)]
88: pub enum DetectError {
89:     /// `GitHubSource::with_base_url` rejected the (owner, repo, api_url) tuple — usually a
90:     /// malformed base URL.
91:     SourceCreation(AttestationError),
92:     /// The attestations endpoint returned an error (403 rate-limit, 5xx, network failure).
93:     Fetch(AttestationError),
94: }
95: 
96: impl std::fmt::Display for DetectError {
97:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
98:         match self {
99:             DetectError::SourceCreation(e) => write!(f, "{e}"),
100:             DetectError::Fetch(e) => write!(f, "{e}"),
101:         }
102:     }
103: }
104: 
105: impl std::error::Error for DetectError {
106:     fn source(&self) -> Option<&(dyn std::error::Error + 'static)> {
107:         match self {
108:             DetectError::SourceCreation(e) => Some(e),
109:             DetectError::Fetch(e) => Some(e),
110:         }
111:     }
112: }
113: 
114: /// Probe the GitHub attestation API for the given digest without downloading the artifact.
115: ///
116: /// Returns `Ok(true)` if any attestations exist for the digest. Used at lock time to decide
117: /// whether `ProvenanceType::GithubAttestations` should be recorded before committing to a
118: /// full download + verify.
119: pub async fn detect_attestations(
120:     owner: &str,
121:     repo: &str,
122:     api_url: &str,
123:     digest: &str,
124: ) -> Result<bool, DetectError> {
125:     let token = resolve_token_for_wrapper(Some(api_url));
126:     let source = GitHubSource::with_base_url(owner, repo, token.as_deref(), api_url)
127:         .map_err(DetectError::SourceCreation)?;
128:     let artifact_ref = ArtifactRef::from_digest(digest);
129:     let attestations = source
130:         .fetch_attestations(&artifact_ref)
131:         .await
132:         .map_err(DetectError::Fetch)?;
133:     Ok(!attestations.is_empty())
134: }
135: 
136: /// Verify SLSA provenance for an already-downloaded artifact. Passthrough — no token needed.
137: pub async fn verify_slsa_provenance(
138:     artifact_path: &Path,
139:     provenance_path: &Path,
140:     min_level: u8,
141: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
142:     mise_sigstore::verify_slsa_provenance(artifact_path, provenance_path, min_level).await
143: }
144: 
145: /// Verify a keyless Cosign signature or bundle. Passthrough — no token needed.
146: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature(
147:     artifact_path: &Path,
148:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
149: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
150:     mise_sigstore::verify_cosign_signature(artifact_path, sig_or_bundle_path).await
151: }
152: 
153: /// Verify a Cosign signature against a public key. Passthrough — no token needed.
154: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature_with_key(
155:     artifact_path: &Path,
156:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
157:     public_key_path: &Path,
158: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
159:     mise_sigstore::verify_cosign_signature_with_key(
160:         artifact_path,
161:         sig_or_bundle_path,
162:         public_key_path,
163:     )
164:     .await
165: }
166: 
167: #[cfg(test)]
168: mod tests {
169:     use super::*;
170:     use crate::env as mise_env;
171: 
172:     const TOKEN_ENV_VARS: &[&str] = &[
173:         "MISE_GITHUB_TOKEN",
174:         "GITHUB_API_TOKEN",
175:         "GITHUB_TOKEN",
176:         "MISE_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN",
177:     ];
178: 
179:     /// RAII guard: snapshots the tracked token env vars on construction, clears them for the
180:     /// test body, and restores the original values on drop — including when a test panics.
181:     struct TokenEnvGuard {
182:         saved: Vec<(&'static str, Option<String>)>,
183:     }
184: 
185:     impl TokenEnvGuard {
186:         fn new() -> Self {
187:             let saved: Vec<_> = TOKEN_ENV_VARS
188:                 .iter()
189:                 .map(|name| (*name, std::env::var(name).ok()))
190:                 .collect();
191:             for name in TOKEN_ENV_VARS {
192:                 mise_env::remove_var(name);
193:             }
194:             Self { saved }
195:         }
196:     }
197: 
198:     impl Drop for TokenEnvGuard {
199:         fn drop(&mut self) {
200:             for (name, value) in std::mem::take(&mut self.saved) {
201:                 match value {
202:                     Some(v) => mise_env::set_var(name, v),
203:                     None => mise_env::remove_var(name),
204:                 }
205:             }
206:         }
207:     }
208: 
209:     #[test]
210:     fn test_resolve_token_wrapper_uses_env_var_with_default_url() {
211:         let _lock = crate::github::TEST_ENV_LOCK.lock().unwrap();
212:         let _env = TokenEnvGuard::new();
213:         mise_env::set_var("GITHUB_TOKEN", "ghp_wrapper_default");
214: 
215:         let resolved = resolve_token_for_wrapper(None);
216:         assert_eq!(
217:             resolved.as_deref(),
218:             Some("ghp_wrapper_default"),
219:             "env var should flow through the wrapper with the default API URL"
220:         );
221:     }
222: 
223:     #[test]
224:     fn test_resolve_token_wrapper_uses_env_var_with_explicit_api_url() {
225:         let _lock = crate::github::TEST_ENV_LOCK.lock().unwrap();
226:         let _env = TokenEnvGuard::new();
227:         mise_env::set_var("MISE_GITHUB_TOKEN", "ghp_explicit_api");
228: 
229:         let resolved = resolve_token_for_wrapper(Some(crate::github::API_URL));
230:         assert_eq!(
231:             resolved.as_deref(),
232:             Some("ghp_explicit_api"),
233:             "explicit api.github.com URL should resolve identically to the default"
234:         );
235:     }
236: 
237:     #[test]
238:     fn test_resolve_token_wrapper_respects_enterprise_api_url() {
239:         let _lock = crate::github::TEST_ENV_LOCK.lock().unwrap();
240:         let _env = TokenEnvGuard::new();
241:         mise_env::set_var("GITHUB_TOKEN", "ghp_public_only");
242:         mise_env::set_var("MISE_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN", "ghp_enterprise_only");
243: 
244:         // An enterprise API URL must parse and route to the enterprise token, proving the
245:         // wrapper passes a full URL (not a bare hostname) to `resolve_token_for_api_url`.
246:         let resolved =
247:             resolve_token_for_wrapper(Some("https://github.enterprise.example.com/api/v3"));
248:         assert_eq!(
249:             resolved.as_deref(),
250:             Some("ghp_enterprise_only"),
251:             "enterprise api_url should resolve the enterprise token, not the public one"
252:         );
253: 
254:         // And the default (None) must still pick the public token.
255:         let resolved_default = resolve_token_for_wrapper(None);
256:         assert_eq!(
257:             resolved_default.as_deref(),
258:             Some("ghp_public_only"),
259:             "default api_url should still resolve the public token"
260:         );
261:     }
262: 
263:     /// Guard that seeds the `github_tokens.toml` test override and clears it on drop.
264:     struct TokensFileOverrideGuard;
265: 
266:     impl TokensFileOverrideGuard {
267:         fn set(host: &str, token: &str) -> Self {
268:             let mut map = std::collections::HashMap::new();
269:             map.insert(host.to_string(), token.to_string());
270:             *crate::github::test_support::TOKENS_FILE_OVERRIDE
271:                 .write()
272:                 .unwrap() = Some(map);
273:             Self
274:         }
275:     }
276: 
277:     impl Drop for TokensFileOverrideGuard {
278:         fn drop(&mut self) {
279:             *crate::github::test_support::TOKENS_FILE_OVERRIDE
280:                 .write()
281:                 .unwrap() = None;
282:         }
283:     }
284: 
285:     #[test]
286:     fn test_resolve_token_wrapper_uses_github_tokens_toml_source() {
287:         // Proves the wrapper delegates all the way through `resolve_token` to the
288:         // non-env-var sources — here, the `github_tokens.toml` path (source #4). Without
289:         // this, a future regression could short-circuit on env vars and silently pass all
290:         // prior tests.
291:         let _lock = crate::github::TEST_ENV_LOCK.lock().unwrap();
292:         let _env = TokenEnvGuard::new();
293:         let _tokens_file = TokensFileOverrideGuard::set("github.com", "ghp_from_tokens_file");
294: 
295:         let resolved = resolve_token_for_wrapper(None);
296:         assert_eq!(
297:             resolved.as_deref(),
298:             Some("ghp_from_tokens_file"),
299:             "wrapper should resolve tokens from github_tokens.toml when env vars are empty"
300:         );
301:     }
302: }

(End of file - total 302 lines)

1: use std::path::Path;
2: 
3: use async_trait::async_trait;
4: use base64::Engine;
5: use reqwest::header::{AUTHORIZATION, HeaderMap, HeaderValue, USER_AGENT};
6: use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
7: use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
8: use sigstore_verify::VerificationPolicy;
9: use sigstore_verify::trust_root::{SigstoreInstance, TrustedRoot};
10: use sigstore_verify::types::{Bundle, DerCertificate, DerPublicKey, SignatureBytes};
11: use thiserror::Error;

14: const GITHUB_API_URL: &str = "https://api.github.com";
15: const USER_AGENT_VALUE: &str = "mise-sigstore/0.1.0";
16: 
17: #[derive(Debug, Error)]
18: pub enum AttestationError {
19:     #[error("API error: {0}")]
20:     Api(String),
21:     #[error("Verification failed: {0}")]
22:     Verification(String),
23:     #[error("Unsupported attestation format: {0}")]
24:     UnsupportedFormat(String),
25:     #[error("No attestations found")]
26:     NoAttestations,
27:     #[error("IO error: {0}")]
28:     Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
29:     #[error("HTTP error: {0}")]
30:     Http(#[from] reqwest::Error),
31:     #[error("JSON error: {0}")]
32:     Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),
33:     #[error("Sigstore error: {0}")]
34:     Sigstore(String),
35: }
36: 
37: impl From<sigstore_verify::Error> for AttestationError {
38:     fn from(err: sigstore_verify::Error) -> Self {
39:         AttestationError::Sigstore(err.to_string())
40:     }
41: }
42: 
43: impl From<sigstore_verify::types::Error> for AttestationError {
44:     fn from(err: sigstore_verify::types::Error) -> Self {
45:         AttestationError::Sigstore(err.to_string())
46:     }
47: }
48: 
49: impl From<sigstore_verify::trust_root::Error> for AttestationError {
50:     fn from(err: sigstore_verify::trust_root::Error) -> Self {
51:         AttestationError::Sigstore(err.to_string())
52:     }
53: }
54: 
55: pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, AttestationError>;
56: 
57: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
58: pub struct ArtifactRef {
59:     digest: String,
60: }
61: 
62: impl ArtifactRef {
63:     pub fn from_digest(digest: &str) -> Self {
64:         if digest.contains(':') {
65:             Self {
66:                 digest: digest.to_string(),
67:             }
68:         } else {
69:             Self {
70:                 digest: format!("sha256:{digest}"),
71:             }
72:         }
73:     }
74: }
75: 
76: #[async_trait]
77: pub trait AttestationSource {
78:     async fn fetch_attestations(&self, artifact: &ArtifactRef) -> Result<Vec<Attestation>>;
79: }
80: 
81: pub mod sources {
82:     pub use crate::{ArtifactRef, AttestationSource};
83: 
84:     pub mod github {
85:         pub use crate::GitHubSource;
86:     }
87: }
88: 
89: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
90: pub struct GitHubSource {
91:     client: AttestationClient,
92:     owner: String,
93:     repo: String,
94: }
95: 
96: impl GitHubSource {
97:     pub fn new(owner: &str, repo: &str, token: Option<&str>) -> Result<Self> {
98:         let mut builder = AttestationClient::builder();
99:         if let Some(token) = token {
100:             builder = builder.github_token(token);
101:         }
102:         Ok(Self {
103:             client: builder.build()?,
104:             owner: owner.to_string(),
105:             repo: repo.to_string(),
106:         })
107:     }
108: 
109:     pub fn with_base_url(
110:         owner: &str,
111:         repo: &str,
112:         token: Option<&str>,
113:         base_url: &str,
114:     ) -> Result<Self> {
115:         let mut builder = AttestationClient::builder().base_url(base_url);
116:         if let Some(token) = token {
117:             builder = builder.github_token(token);
118:         }
119:         Ok(Self {
120:             client: builder.build()?,
121:             owner: owner.to_string(),
122:             repo: repo.to_string(),
123:         })
124:     }
125: }

127: #[async_trait]
128: impl AttestationSource for GitHubSource {
129:     async fn fetch_attestations(&self, artifact: &ArtifactRef) -> Result<Vec<Attestation>> {
130:         self.client
131:             .fetch_attestations(FetchParams {
132:                 owner: self.owner.clone(),
133:                 repo: Some(format!("{}/{}", self.owner, self.repo)),
134:                 digest: artifact.digest.clone(),
135:                 limit: 30,
136:                 predicate_type: None,
137:             })
138:             .await
139:     }
140: }
141: 
142: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
143: pub struct AttestationClient {
144:     client: reqwest::Client,
145:     base_url: String,
146:     github_token: Option<String>,
147: }
148: 
149: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
150: pub struct AttestationClientBuilder {
151:     base_url: Option<String>,
152:     github_token: Option<String>,
153: }
154: 
155: impl AttestationClientBuilder {
156:     pub fn base_url(mut self, url: &str) -> Self {
157:         self.base_url = Some(url.trim_end_matches('/').to_string());
158:         self
159:     }
160: 
161:     pub fn github_token(mut self, token: &str) -> Self {
162:         self.github_token = Some(token.to_string());
163:         self
164:     }
165: 
166:     pub fn build(self) -> Result<AttestationClient> {
167:         let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();
168:         headers.insert(USER_AGENT, HeaderValue::from_static(USER_AGENT_VALUE));
169:         let client = reqwest::Client::builder()
170:             .default_headers(headers)
171:             .build()?;
172: 
173:         Ok(AttestationClient {
174:             client,
175:             base_url: self.base_url.unwrap_or_else(|| GITHUB_API_URL.to_string()),
176:             github_token: self.github_token,
177:         })
178:     }
179: }
180: 
181: #[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
182: pub struct FetchParams {
183:     pub owner: String,
184:     pub repo: Option<String>,
185:     pub digest: String,
186:     pub limit: usize,
187:     pub predicate_type: Option<String>,
188: }
189: 
190: #[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
191: struct AttestationsResponse {
192:     attestations: Vec<Attestation>,
193: }
194: 
195: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
196: pub struct Attestation {
197:     bundle: Option<serde_json::Value>,
198:     bundle_url: Option<String>,
199: }
200: 
201: impl AttestationClient {
202:     pub fn builder() -> AttestationClientBuilder {
203:         AttestationClientBuilder::default()
204:     }
205: 
206:     fn github_headers(&self, url: &str) -> Result<HeaderMap> {
207:         let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();
208:         let base_with_slash = format!("{}/", self.base_url);
209:         if url == self.base_url || url.starts_with(&base_with_slash) {
210:             if let Some(token) = &self.github_token {
211:                 headers.insert(
212:                     AUTHORIZATION,
213:                     HeaderValue::from_str(&format!("Bearer {token}"))
214:                         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Api(e.to_string()))?,
215:                 );
216:             }
217:             headers.insert(
218:                 "x-github-api-version",
219:                 HeaderValue::from_static("2022-11-28"),
220:             );
221:         }
222:         Ok(headers)
223:     }
224: 
225:     pub async fn fetch_attestations(&self, params: FetchParams) -> Result<Vec<Attestation>> {
226:         let url = if let Some(repo) = &params.repo {
227:             format!(
228:                 "{}/repos/{repo}/attestations/{}",
229:                 self.base_url, params.digest
230:             )
231:         } else {
232:             format!(
233:                 "{}/orgs/{}/attestations/{}",
234:                 self.base_url, params.owner, params.digest
235:             )
236:         };
237: 
238:         let mut query_params = vec![("per_page", params.limit.to_string())];
239:         if let Some(predicate_type) = &params.predicate_type {
240:             query_params.push(("predicate_type", predicate_type.clone()));
241:         }
242:         let url = reqwest::Url::parse_with_params(&url, query_params)
243:             .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Api(format!("Invalid GitHub attestations URL: {e}")))?;
244: 
245:         let response = self
246:             .client
247:             .get(url.clone())
248:             .headers(self.github_headers(url.as_str())?)
249:             .send()
250:             .await?;
251: 
252:         if response.status() == reqwest::StatusCode::NOT_FOUND {
253:             return Ok(vec![]);
254:         }
255:         if !response.status().is_success() {
256:             let status = response.status();
257:             let body = response
258:                 .text()
259:                 .await
260:                 .unwrap_or_else(|_| "Unknown error".to_string());
261:             return Err(AttestationError::Api(format!(
262:                 "GitHub API returned {status}: {body}"
263:             )));
264:         }
265: 
266:         let response: AttestationsResponse = response.json().await?;
267:         let mut attestations = Vec::new();
268:         for attestation in response.attestations {
269:             if attestation.bundle.is_some() {
270:                 attestations.push(attestation);
271:             } else if let Some(bundle_url) = &attestation.bundle_url {
272:                 let bundle = self.fetch_bundle_url(bundle_url).await?;
273:                 attestations.push(Attestation {
274:                     bundle: Some(bundle),
275:                     bundle_url: Some(bundle_url.clone()),
276:                 });
277:             }
278:         }
279:         Ok(attestations)
280:     }
281: 
282:     async fn fetch_bundle_url(&self, bundle_url: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
283:         let response = self
284:             .client
285:             .get(bundle_url)
286:             .headers(self.github_headers(bundle_url)?)
287:             .send()
288:             .await?;
289:         if !response.status().is_success() {
290:             return Err(AttestationError::Api(format!(
291:                 "bundle URL returned {}",
292:                 response.status()
293:             )));
294:         }
295:         if is_snappy_content_type(&response) {
296:             let bytes = response.bytes().await?;
297:             let decompressed = snap::raw::Decoder::new()
298:                 .decompress_vec(&bytes)
299:                 .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Api(format!("Snappy decompression failed: {e}")))?;
300:             serde_json::from_slice(&decompressed).map_err(AttestationError::Json)
301:         } else {
302:             response.json().await.map_err(AttestationError::Http)
303:         }
304:     }
305: }

308:     artifact_path: &Path,

312:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
313: ) -> Result<bool> {
314:     verify_github_attestation_inner(artifact_path, owner, repo, token, signer_workflow, None).await

318:     artifact_path: &Path,
319:     owner: &str,
320:     repo: &str,
321:     token: Option<&str>,
322:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,

326:         artifact_path,

336: async fn verify_github_attestation_inner(
337:     artifact_path: &Path,
338:     owner: &str,
339:     repo: &str,
340:     token: Option<&str>,
341:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
342:     base_url: Option<&str>,
343: ) -> Result<bool> {
344:     let mut builder = AttestationClient::builder();
345:     if let Some(token) = token {
346:         builder = builder.github_token(token);
347:     }
348:     if let Some(base_url) = base_url {
349:         builder = builder.base_url(base_url);
350:     }
351:     let client = builder.build()?;
352:     let digest = calculate_file_digest_async(artifact_path).await?;
353:     let attestations = client
354:         .fetch_attestations(FetchParams {
355:             owner: owner.to_string(),
356:             repo: Some(format!("{owner}/{repo}")),
357:             digest: format!("sha256:{digest}"),
358:             limit: 30,
359:             predicate_type: None,
360:         })
361:         .await?;
362: 
363:     if attestations.is_empty() {
364:         return Err(AttestationError::NoAttestations);
365:     }
366: 
367:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
368:     let mut trust_roots = TrustRoots::default();
369:     verify_attestation_bundles(&attestations, &artifact, signer_workflow, &mut trust_roots).await
370: }

372: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature(
373:     artifact_path: &Path,
374:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
375: ) -> Result<bool> {
376:     let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(sig_or_bundle_path).await?;
377:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
378:     let mut trust_roots = TrustRoots::default();
379:     if let Ok(bundle) = Bundle::from_json(&content) {
380:         let trusted_root = trust_roots.for_bundle(&bundle).await?;
381:         verify_bundle(&artifact, &bundle, None, trusted_root)?;
382:         return Ok(true);
383:     }
384:     // Legacy cosign v1 bundle (`{base64Signature, cert, rekorBundle}`).
385:     // sigstore-verify only consumes the modern bundle shape, so we verify
386:     // these manually: chain-validate the embedded cert against Sigstore
387:     // Fulcio, then ECDSA-verify the signature over the artifact bytes.
388:     let trusted_root = trust_roots.sigstore_root().await?;
389:     verify_legacy_cosign_bundle(&artifact, &content, trusted_root)?;
390:     Ok(true)
391: }
392: 
393: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature_with_key(
394:     artifact_path: &Path,
395:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
396:     public_key_path: &Path,
397: ) -> Result<bool> {
398:     let key_pem = tokio::fs::read_to_string(public_key_path).await?;
399:     let public_key = DerPublicKey::from_pem(&key_pem)?;
400: 
401:     // Read the file once, propagating real I/O errors. Only a JSON-parse
402:     // failure means "this isn't a sigstore bundle, treat it as a raw `.sig`."
403:     let raw_bytes = tokio::fs::read(sig_or_bundle_path).await?;
404:     let bundle = std::str::from_utf8(&raw_bytes)
405:         .ok()
406:         .and_then(|content| Bundle::from_json(content).ok());
407:     if let Some(bundle) = bundle {
408:         // Bundle path: needs the trust root for tlog (Rekor) verification.
409:         let trusted_root = production_trusted_root().await?;
410:         let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
411:         let result = sigstore_verify::verify_with_key(
412:             artifact.as_slice(),
413:             &bundle,
414:             &public_key,
415:             &trusted_root,
416:         )?;
417:         if !result.success {
418:             return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
419:                 "sigstore verification returned false".to_string(),
420:             ));
421:         }
422:         return Ok(true);
423:     }
424: 
425:     // Raw `.sig` path: only needs the local public key — no network access.
426:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
427:     let signature = decode_cosign_signature(&raw_bytes);
428:     verify_raw_signature(&artifact, &signature, &public_key)?;
429:     Ok(true)
430: }

432: pub async fn verify_slsa_provenance(
433:     artifact_path: &Path,
434:     provenance_path: &Path,
435:     min_level: u8,
436: ) -> Result<bool> {
437:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
438:     let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(provenance_path).await?;
439:     let mut errors = Vec::new();
440:     let mut trust_roots = TrustRoots::default();
441: 
442:     let mut candidates: Vec<&str> = content
443:         .lines()
444:         .map(str::trim)
445:         .filter(|line| !line.is_empty())
446:         .collect();
447:     let trimmed = content.trim();
448:     if !trimmed.is_empty() && !candidates.contains(&trimmed) {
449:         candidates.push(trimmed);
450:     }
451: 
452:     for candidate in candidates {
453:         // Bundle::from_json failure falls through to the DSSE envelope path.
454:         if let Ok(bundle) = Bundle::from_json(candidate) {
455:             let result = match trust_roots.for_bundle(&bundle).await {
456:                 Ok(root) => verify_bundle(&artifact, &bundle, None, root)
457:                     .and_then(|_| verify_bundle_slsa(&bundle, &artifact, min_level)),
458:                 Err(e) => Err(e),
459:             };
460:             match result {
461:                 Ok(()) => return Ok(true),
462:                 Err(e) => errors.push(e),
463:             }
464:             continue;
465:         }
466:         // slsa-github-generator and goreleaser write the provenance as a raw
467:         // DSSE envelope (`*.intoto.jsonl`) rather than a sigstore bundle —
468:         // there is no `verificationMaterial`, so `Bundle::from_json` rejects
469:         // it. Match the in-toto payload manually and check artifact digest +
470:         // SLSA predicate without going through sigstore-verify. Use the public
471:         // Sigstore trust root since slsa-github-generator certs are issued by
472:         // Sigstore Fulcio.
473:         let result = match trust_roots.sigstore_root().await {
474:             Ok(root) => verify_intoto_envelope(candidate, &artifact, min_level, root),
475:             Err(e) => Err(e),
476:         };
477:         match result {
478:             Ok(()) => return Ok(true),
479:             Err(e) => errors.push(e),
480:         }
481:     }
482: 
483:     collapse_slsa_errors(errors, || {
484:         "File does not contain valid attestations or SLSA provenance".to_string()
485:     })
486: }
487: 
488: fn verify_intoto_envelope(
489:     line: &str,
490:     artifact: &[u8],
491:     min_level: u8,
492:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
493: ) -> Result<()> {
494:     let envelope: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| {
495:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!("not a JSON DSSE envelope: {e}"))
496:     })?;
497:     let payload_type = envelope
498:         .get("payloadType")
499:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
500:         .unwrap_or_default();
501:     if payload_type != "application/vnd.in-toto+json" {
502:         return Err(AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!(
503:             "unsupported DSSE payloadType: {payload_type}"
504:         )));
505:     }
506:     let payload_b64 = envelope
507:         .get("payload")
508:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
509:         .ok_or_else(|| {
510:             AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat("DSSE envelope missing payload".to_string())
511:         })?;
512:     let payload = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
513:         .decode(payload_b64.as_bytes())
514:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 payload: {e}")))?;
515: 
516:     // DSSE signature verification. The envelope's signatures sign the
517:     // Pre-Authentication Encoding of the payload, not the payload itself.
518:     // Without this check, anyone able to substitute the provenance file could
519:     // forge a passing attestation just by including the artifact's digest in
520:     // the in-toto subject list.
521:     //
522:     // Each signature embeds the Sigstore Fulcio leaf cert that signed it
523:     // (slsa-github-generator format). We chain-validate that cert against the
524:     // public Sigstore trust root, then verify the signature against the PAE
525:     // using the cert's public key. A self-signed forged cert would be
526:     // rejected at the chain step. Bundles in the modern sigstore format
527:     // (which carry tlog/TSA) take the strict `verify_bundle` path above.
528:     let signatures = envelope
529:         .get("signatures")
530:         .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
531:         .ok_or_else(|| {
532:             AttestationError::Verification("DSSE envelope missing signatures".to_string())
533:         })?;
534:     if signatures.is_empty() {
535:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
536:             "DSSE envelope has no signatures".to_string(),
537:         ));
538:     }
539:     let pae = sigstore_verify::types::pae(payload_type, &payload);
540:     let mut sig_errors = Vec::new();
541:     let mut verified = false;
542:     for sig in signatures {
543:         match verify_dsse_signature(sig, &pae, trusted_root) {
544:             Ok(()) => {
545:                 verified = true;
546:                 break;
547:             }
548:             Err(e) => sig_errors.push(e.to_string()),
549:         }
550:     }
551:     if !verified {
552:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
553:             "no valid DSSE signature: {}",
554:             join_error_strings(sig_errors, || "no signatures could be verified".to_string())
555:         )));
556:     }
557: 
558:     verify_intoto_payload(&payload, artifact, min_level)
559: }
560: 
561: /// Verify a legacy cosign v1 keyless bundle (`{base64Signature, cert, rekorBundle}`).
562: ///
563: /// Cosign 2.x and earlier `cosign sign-blob --bundle` writes this format. The
564: /// modern sigstore Bundle (with `verificationMaterial`/`messageSignature`)
565: /// replaces it, but tools like goreleaser still produce v1 bundles in their
566: /// release artifacts. Verification mirrors what we do for raw DSSE envelopes:
567: /// decode the embedded Fulcio cert (PEM in `cert`), chain-validate it against
568: /// the public Sigstore trust root, then ECDSA-verify `base64Signature` over
569: /// the raw artifact bytes with the cert's public key.
570: ///
571: /// The Rekor `SignedEntryTimestamp` and the artifact hash recorded in the
572: /// rekord entry aren't independently re-checked here — re-verifying them
573: /// would require a Rekor public key lookup and adds little: the cert+sig
574: /// step already cryptographically binds the signer to the artifact bytes,
575: /// which is what every downstream consumer cares about.
576: fn verify_legacy_cosign_bundle(
577:     artifact: &[u8],
578:     bundle_json: &str,
579:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
580: ) -> Result<()> {
581:     let value: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(bundle_json).map_err(|e| {
582:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!("not a sigstore or cosign bundle: {e}"))
583:     })?;
584:     let cert_b64 = value.get("cert").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).ok_or_else(|| {
585:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat("legacy cosign bundle missing cert".to_string())
586:     })?;
587:     let sig_b64 = value
588:         .get("base64Signature")
589:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
590:         .ok_or_else(|| {
591:             AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(
592:                 "legacy cosign bundle missing base64Signature".to_string(),
593:             )
594:         })?;
595: 
596:     let cert_pem_bytes = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
597:         .decode(cert_b64.as_bytes())
598:         .map_err(|e| {
599:             AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 cert in legacy bundle: {e}"))
600:         })?;
601:     let cert_pem = std::str::from_utf8(&cert_pem_bytes).map_err(|e| {
602:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("legacy cosign cert is not UTF-8 PEM: {e}"))
603:     })?;
604:     let cert = DerCertificate::from_pem(cert_pem)?;
605:     verify_cert_chain(cert.as_bytes(), trusted_root)?;
606: 
607:     let sig_bytes = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
608:         .decode(sig_b64.as_bytes())
609:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 signature: {e}")))?;
610:     let spki_der = extract_spki_der(cert.as_bytes())?;
611:     let public_key = DerPublicKey::new(spki_der);
612:     verify_raw_signature(artifact, &sig_bytes, &public_key)
613: }
614: 
615: fn verify_dsse_signature(
616:     sig: &serde_json::Value,
617:     pae: &[u8],
618:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
619: ) -> Result<()> {
620:     let cert_pem = sig.get("cert").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).ok_or_else(|| {
621:         AttestationError::Verification("DSSE signature missing cert field".to_string())
622:     })?;
623:     let sig_b64 = sig.get("sig").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).ok_or_else(|| {
624:         AttestationError::Verification("DSSE signature missing sig field".to_string())
625:     })?;
626:     let sig_bytes = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
627:         .decode(sig_b64.as_bytes())
628:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 signature: {e}")))?;
629:     let cert = DerCertificate::from_pem(cert_pem)?;
630:     // Chain-validate the embedded cert before trusting its public key.
631:     verify_cert_chain(cert.as_bytes(), trusted_root)?;
632:     let spki_der = extract_spki_der(cert.as_bytes())?;
633:     let public_key = DerPublicKey::new(spki_der);
634:     verify_raw_signature(pae, &sig_bytes, &public_key)
635: }
636: 
637: fn verify_intoto_payload(payload: &[u8], artifact: &[u8], min_level: u8) -> Result<()> {
638:     let statement: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(payload).map_err(|e| {
639:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("Failed to parse SLSA payload: {e}"))
640:     })?;
641:     let predicate_type = statement
642:         .get("predicateType")
643:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
644:         .unwrap_or_default();
645:     if !predicate_type.starts_with("https://slsa.dev/provenance/") {
646:         return Err(AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!(
647:             "Not an SLSA provenance predicate: {predicate_type}"
648:         )));
649:     }
650:     if min_level > 1 {
651:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
652:             "SLSA level {min_level} verification is not supported by the native adapter"
653:         )));
654:     }
655:     let artifact_digest = hex::encode(Sha256::digest(artifact));
656:     let subjects = statement
657:         .get("subject")
658:         .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
659:         .ok_or_else(|| {
660:             AttestationError::Verification("SLSA statement missing subject array".to_string())
661:         })?;
662:     let matches_subject = subjects.iter().any(|subject| {
663:         subject
664:             .get("digest")
665:             .and_then(|d| d.get("sha256"))
666:             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
667:             .is_some_and(|sha| sha.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&artifact_digest))
668:     });
669:     if !matches_subject {
670:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
671:             "artifact sha256 {artifact_digest} not found in SLSA statement subjects"
672:         )));
673:     }
674:     Ok(())
675: }
676: 
677: fn collapse_slsa_errors(
678:     errors: Vec<AttestationError>,
679:     default: impl FnOnce() -> String,
680: ) -> Result<bool> {
681:     let unsupported_format = errors
682:         .iter()
683:         .all(|error| matches!(error, AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(_)));
684:     let message = join_error_strings(
685:         errors.into_iter().map(|error| error.to_string()).collect(),
686:         default,
687:     );
688:     Err(if unsupported_format {
689:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(message)
690:     } else {
691:         AttestationError::Verification(message)
692:     })
693: }
694: 
695: fn join_error_strings(errors: Vec<String>, default: impl FnOnce() -> String) -> String {
696:     let mut errors = errors
697:         .into_iter()
698:         .filter(|error| !error.trim().is_empty())
699:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
700:     errors.dedup();
701:     if errors.is_empty() {
702:         default()
703:     } else {
704:         errors.join("; ")
705:     }
706: }
707: 
708: async fn verify_attestation_bundles(
709:     attestations: &[Attestation],
710:     artifact: &[u8],
711:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
712:     trust_roots: &mut TrustRoots,
713: ) -> Result<bool> {
714:     let mut errors = Vec::new();
715:     for attestation in attestations {
716:         let Some(bundle_value) = &attestation.bundle else {
717:             continue;
718:         };
719:         let bundle = match serde_json::from_value::<Bundle>(bundle_value.clone()) {
720:             Ok(bundle) => bundle,
721:             Err(e) => {
722:                 errors.push(e.to_string());
723:                 continue;
724:             }
725:         };
726:         let trusted_root = match trust_roots.for_bundle(&bundle).await {
727:             Ok(root) => root,
728:             Err(e) => {
729:                 errors.push(e.to_string());
730:                 continue;
731:             }
732:         };
733:         match verify_bundle(artifact, &bundle, signer_workflow, trusted_root) {
734:             Ok(()) => return Ok(true),
735:             Err(e) => errors.push(e.to_string()),
736:         }
737:     }
738: 
739:     Err(AttestationError::Verification(join_error_strings(
740:         errors,
741:         || "No valid attestations found".to_string(),
742:     )))
743: }
744: 
745: fn is_snappy_content_type(response: &reqwest::Response) -> bool {
746:     response
747:         .headers()
748:         .get(reqwest::header::CONTENT_TYPE)
749:         .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
750:         .and_then(|content_type| content_type.split(';').next())
751:         .is_some_and(|content_type| content_type.trim() == "application/x-snappy")
752: }
753: 
754: fn verify_bundle(
755:     artifact: &[u8],
756:     bundle: &Bundle,
757:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
758:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
759: ) -> Result<()> {
760:     let mut policy = VerificationPolicy::default();
761:     // sigstore-verify's default policy *requires* an inclusion proof when
762:     // `verify_tlog` is on. GitHub artifact attestations and TSA-only bundles
763:     // never carry one, so we'd reject them outright. Skip tlog only when the
764:     // bundle has no inclusion proof — public-Sigstore cosign bundles, which do
765:     // ship a Rekor inclusion proof, still get full tlog verification (Rekor
766:     // checkpoint signature, SET, inclusion-proof Merkle path).
767:     if !bundle.has_inclusion_proof() {
768:         policy = policy.skip_tlog();
769:     }
770:     // GitHub-internal leaf certs don't carry an SCT extension (GitHub's CA
771:     // doesn't log to public CT). `skip_sct` keeps full certificate-chain
772:     // validation against the GitHub trust root's Fulcio certs but turns off
773:     // the SCT check, which is exactly what GitHub artifact attestations need.
774:     if is_github_internal_certificate(bundle) {
775:         policy = policy.skip_sct();
776:     }
777:     let result = sigstore_verify::verify(artifact, bundle, &policy, trusted_root)?;
778:     if !result.success {
779:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
780:             "sigstore verification returned false".to_string(),
781:         ));
782:     }
783: 
784:     verify_signer_workflow_identity(result.identity.as_deref(), signer_workflow)?;
785: 
786:     Ok(())
787: }
788: 
789: fn is_github_internal_certificate(bundle: &Bundle) -> bool {
790:     bundle
791:         .signing_certificate()
792:         .map(|cert| cert_issuer_organization(cert.as_bytes()).as_deref() == Some("GitHub, Inc."))
793:         .unwrap_or(false)
794: }
795: 
796: /// Verify that a leaf certificate chains to one of the trust root's CA certs.
797: ///
798: /// Used for raw DSSE envelopes (`*.intoto.jsonl` from slsa-github-generator),
799: /// which don't have the bundle structure sigstore-verify expects, so we can't
800: /// delegate to `sigstore_verify::verify`. GitHub-internal bundles go through
801: /// sigstore-verify directly with `skip_sct`.
802: ///
803: /// webpki performs the same chain-building, ECDSA/RSA signature checks, and
804: /// CODE_SIGNING EKU enforcement as sigstore-verify, just without the SCT step.
805: ///
806: /// Validation time is the leaf cert's `notAfter`. Fulcio leaves are
807: /// short-lived (~10 min) so by `now()` they're already expired and we have no
808: /// independently verified time source here. Using `notAfter` rather than
809: /// `notBefore` is the stricter choice: it catches any intermediate CA whose
810: /// own validity ends before the leaf's, which would otherwise slip through.
811: fn verify_cert_chain(leaf_der: &[u8], trusted_root: &TrustedRoot) -> Result<()> {
812:     use rustls_pki_types::{CertificateDer, UnixTime};
813:     use webpki::{ALL_VERIFICATION_ALGS, EndEntityCert, KeyUsage, anchor_from_trusted_cert};
814:     use x509_cert::Certificate;
815:     use x509_cert::der::Decode;
816: 
817:     let leaf = Certificate::from_der(leaf_der).map_err(|e| {
818:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to parse leaf certificate: {e}"))
819:     })?;
820:     let not_after = leaf
821:         .tbs_certificate
822:         .validity
823:         .not_after
824:         .to_unix_duration()
825:         .as_secs();
826:     let validation_time = UnixTime::since_unix_epoch(std::time::Duration::from_secs(not_after));
827: 
828:     let all_certs = trusted_root.fulcio_certs().map_err(|e| {
829:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to load CA certs from trust root: {e}"))
830:     })?;
831:     if all_certs.is_empty() {
832:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
833:             "trust root contains no CA certificates".to_string(),
834:         ));
835:     }
836:     // Use every CA cert in the trust root as both a trust anchor and as a
837:     // possible intermediate. `anchor_from_trusted_cert` accepts any parseable
838:     // cert (not just self-signed roots), and that's intentional: we trust the
839:     // whole CA bundle the trust root ships, so it's fine for chain validation
840:     // to terminate at an intermediate rather than walk all the way up to the
841:     // self-signed root. This matches what sigstore-verify does internally.
842:     // The chain itself is still cryptographically verified end-to-end.
843:     let trust_anchors: Vec<_> = all_certs
844:         .iter()
845:         .filter_map(|der| {
846:             anchor_from_trusted_cert(&CertificateDer::from(der.as_ref()))
847:                 .map(|a| a.to_owned())
848:                 .ok()
849:         })
850:         .collect();
851:     if trust_anchors.is_empty() {
852:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
853:             "trust root CA certs are unparseable".to_string(),
854:         ));
855:     }
856:     let intermediate_certs: Vec<CertificateDer<'static>> = all_certs
857:         .iter()
858:         .map(|der| CertificateDer::from(der.as_ref()).into_owned())
859:         .collect();
860: 
861:     let leaf_der_ref = CertificateDer::from(leaf_der);
862:     let leaf_cert = EndEntityCert::try_from(&leaf_der_ref).map_err(|e| {
863:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to parse leaf for chain check: {e}"))
864:     })?;
865: 
866:     // 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3 — id-kp-codeSigning, raw OID bytes (no DER tag/length).
867:     const ID_KP_CODE_SIGNING: &[u8] = &[0x2b, 0x06, 0x01, 0x05, 0x05, 0x07, 0x03, 0x03];
868: 
869:     leaf_cert
870:         .verify_for_usage(
871:             ALL_VERIFICATION_ALGS,
872:             &trust_anchors,
873:             &intermediate_certs,
874:             validation_time,
875:             KeyUsage::required(ID_KP_CODE_SIGNING),
876:             None,
877:             None,
878:         )
879:         .map_err(|e| {
880:             AttestationError::Verification(format!("certificate chain validation failed: {e}"))
881:         })?;
882:     Ok(())
883: }
884: 
885: /// Return the X.509 Issuer's `O` (organizationName) attribute, if present.
886: ///
887: /// Used to dispatch verification policy: certs issued by GitHub's internal
888: /// Fulcio (`O=GitHub, Inc.`) need a separate trust root and a relaxed policy.
889: /// Parses the cert with x509-cert rather than byte-searching the DER, so we
890: /// only match the actual issuer organization field — not arbitrary substrings
891: /// elsewhere in the certificate.
892: fn cert_issuer_organization(cert_der: &[u8]) -> Option<String> {
893:     use x509_cert::Certificate;
894:     use x509_cert::der::Decode;
895:     let cert = Certificate::from_der(cert_der).ok()?;
896:     for rdn in cert.tbs_certificate.issuer.0.iter() {
897:         for atv in rdn.0.iter() {
898:             // 2.5.4.10 = id-at-organizationName
899:             if atv.oid.to_string() == "2.5.4.10" {
900:                 if let Ok(s) = atv.value.decode_as::<String>() {
901:                     return Some(s);
902:                 }
903:                 if let Ok(s) = atv
904:                     .value
905:                     .decode_as::<x509_cert::der::asn1::PrintableStringRef>()
906:                 {
907:                     return Some(s.as_str().to_string());
908:                 }
909:                 if let Ok(s) = atv.value.decode_as::<x509_cert::der::asn1::Utf8StringRef>() {
910:                     return Some(s.as_str().to_string());
911:                 }
912:             }
913:         }
914:     }
915:     None
916: }
917: 
918: /// Extract the SubjectPublicKeyInfo bytes (DER) from an X.509 certificate.
919: fn extract_spki_der(cert_der: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
920:     use x509_cert::Certificate;
921:     use x509_cert::der::{Decode, Encode};
922:     let cert = Certificate::from_der(cert_der)
923:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to parse certificate: {e}")))?;
924:     cert.tbs_certificate
925:         .subject_public_key_info
926:         .to_der()
927:         .map_err(|e| {
928:             AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to encode SubjectPublicKeyInfo: {e}"))
929:         })
930: }

933:     Ok(TrustedRoot::production().await?)
934: }
935: 
936: fn github_trusted_root() -> Result<TrustedRoot> {
937:     Ok(TrustedRoot::from_embedded(SigstoreInstance::GitHub)?)
938: }
939: 
940: /// Per-process cache so we only fetch the Sigstore TUF root or parse the
941: /// embedded GitHub trusted root once per `verify_*` invocation. Each is
942: /// loaded lazily — a verification flow that only ever sees GitHub bundles
943: /// never triggers a network call to the Sigstore TUF CDN, and vice versa.
944: #[derive(Default)]
945: struct TrustRoots {
946:     sigstore: Option<TrustedRoot>,
947:     github: Option<TrustedRoot>,
948: }
949: 
950: impl TrustRoots {
951:     async fn for_bundle(&mut self, bundle: &Bundle) -> Result<&TrustedRoot> {
952:         if is_github_internal_certificate(bundle) {
953:             self.github_root()
954:         } else {
955:             self.sigstore_root().await
956:         }
957:     }
958: 
959:     async fn sigstore_root(&mut self) -> Result<&TrustedRoot> {
960:         if self.sigstore.is_none() {
961:             self.sigstore = Some(production_trusted_root().await?);
962:         }
963:         Ok(self.sigstore.as_ref().unwrap())
964:     }
965: 
966:     fn github_root(&mut self) -> Result<&TrustedRoot> {
967:         if self.github.is_none() {
968:             self.github = Some(github_trusted_root()?);
969:         }
970:         Ok(self.github.as_ref().unwrap())
971:     }
972: }
973: 
974: fn verify_signer_workflow_identity(
975:     identity: Option<&str>,
976:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
977: ) -> Result<()> {
978:     let Some(expected) = signer_workflow else {
979:         return Ok(());
980:     };
981:     let Some(identity) = identity.filter(|identity| !identity.is_empty()) else {
982:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
983:             "Workflow verification failed: expected '{expected}', found no certificate identity"
984:         )));
985:     };
986:     if !identity.contains(expected) {
987:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
988:             "Workflow verification failed: expected '{expected}', found certificate identity: {identity:?}"
989:         )));
990:     }
991:     Ok(())
992: }
993: 
994: /// SLSA-specific checks once `verify_bundle` has cryptographically verified
995: /// the bundle: the DSSE payload is an SLSA provenance statement, the policy
996: /// level is supported, and the artifact's SHA-256 appears in the statement's
997: /// `subject` array. The subject check is the load-bearing part — without it,
998: /// a valid SLSA bundle signed for *some* artifact would accept *any* artifact.
999: fn verify_bundle_slsa(bundle: &Bundle, artifact: &[u8], min_level: u8) -> Result<()> {
1000:     let payload = match &bundle.content {
1001:         sigstore_verify::types::SignatureContent::DsseEnvelope(envelope) => {
1002:             envelope.decode_payload()
1003:         }
1004:         _ => {
1005:             return Err(AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(
1006:                 "SLSA provenance must be a DSSE envelope".to_string(),
1007:             ));
1008:         }
1009:     };
1010:     verify_intoto_payload(&payload, artifact, min_level)
1011: }
1012: 
1013: fn decode_cosign_signature(bytes: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8> {
1014:     let trimmed = String::from_utf8_lossy(bytes).trim().to_string();
1015:     if let Some(decoded) = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
1016:         .decode(trimmed.as_bytes())
1017:         .ok()
1018:         .filter(|_| !trimmed.is_empty())
1019:     {
1020:         return decoded;
1021:     }
1022:     bytes.to_vec()
1023: }
1024: 
1025: fn verify_raw_signature(
1026:     artifact: &[u8],
1027:     signature: &[u8],
1028:     public_key: &DerPublicKey,
1029: ) -> Result<()> {
1030:     use sigstore_verify::crypto::{KeyType, SigningScheme, detect_key_type, verify_signature};
1031: 
1032:     let scheme = match detect_key_type(public_key) {
1033:         KeyType::Ed25519 => SigningScheme::Ed25519,
1034:         KeyType::EcdsaP256 => SigningScheme::EcdsaP256Sha256,
1035:         KeyType::Unknown => {
1036:             return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
1037:                 "unsupported or unrecognized public key type".to_string(),
1038:             ));
1039:         }
1040:     };
1041:     let signature = SignatureBytes::from_bytes(signature);
1042:     verify_signature(public_key, artifact, &signature, scheme)
1043:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("signature verification failed: {e}")))
1044: }

1046: async fn calculate_file_digest_async(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {
1047:     let mut file = tokio::fs::File::open(path).await?;
1048:     let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
1049:     let mut buffer = [0; 8192];
1050:     loop {
1051:         let read = file.read(&mut buffer).await?;
1052:         if read == 0 {
1053:             break;
1054:         }
1055:         hasher.update(&buffer[..read]);
1056:     }
1057:     Ok(hex::encode(hasher.finalize()))
1058: }

state.title: crates/mise-sigstore/src/lib.rs

part.text:
Now I understand the architecture. Let me look at how the github backend and aqua backend actually verify attestations on installed tools. The key functions are:
- `verify_github_attestation` and `verify_github_attestation_with_base_url` in mise-sigstore crate
- They're called via `verify_attestation` in `src/github/sigstore.rs`
- And via `run_github_attestation_check` in `src/backend/aqua.rs` (and probably github.rs)

The scenario: GitHub releases sometimes attest each file inside an archive (e.g., each binary inside a tarball) rather than the archive blob itself. In that case, verifying the archive blob's digest against GitHub's attestation API would fail because the attestation is over the inner file's digest, not the archive's digest.

Currently mise does:
1. Downloads the archive
2. Computes its digest
3. Calls GitHub attestation API with that digest
4. If no attestations → fails (or treats as "no attestations")

For the per-file-in-archive case, we need to:
1. Try archive-level verification first (preserve existing behavior)
2. If that fails, and if the artifact is an archive, extract it and try to verify each file inside the archive
3. Fail closed if neither works

Let me look at how the github backend and aqua backend handle this currently to understand the call sites.

Let me look at the github backend more carefully:

120: /// attempt against a custom api_url will fail. Callers gate on this so users
121: /// don't have to disable `MISE_GITHUB_ATTESTATIONS` globally for GHE tools.
122: fn attestations_supported(api_url: &str) -> bool {
123:     api_url.trim_end_matches('/') == DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL
124: }
125: 
126: /// Status returned from verification attempts
127: enum VerificationStatus {
128:     /// No attestations or provenance found (not an error, tool may not have them)
129:     NoAttestations,
130:     /// An error occurred during verification
131:     Error(String),
132: }
133: 
134: /// Check if an SLSA verification error indicates a format/parsing issue rather than
135: /// an actual verification failure. Some provenance files (e.g., BuildKit raw provenance)
136: /// exist but aren't in a sigstore-verifiable format.
137: ///
138: /// `Sigstore(msg)` covers errors that originated in `sigstore-verify` itself
139: /// (e.g. `missing field 'verificationMaterial'` when the file is a legacy
140: /// cosign v1 bundle that the modern bundle deserializer rejects). Those are
141: /// format mismatches, not signature failures, so we let the caller fall
142: /// back to alternate verification paths.
143: fn is_slsa_format_issue(e: &crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError) -> bool {
144:     match e {
145:         crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::NoAttestations => true,
146:         crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(_) => true,
147:         crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::Verification(msg)
148:         | crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::Sigstore(msg) => {
149:             msg.contains("does not contain valid attestations")
150:                 || msg.contains("No certificate found")
151:                 || msg.contains("neither DSSE envelope nor message signature")
152:                 || msg.contains("missing field")
153:                 || msg.contains("not a sigstore or cosign bundle")
154:                 || msg.contains("not a JSON DSSE envelope")
155:         }
156:         _ => false,
157:     }
158: }
159: 
160: /// Returns install-time-only option keys for GitHub/GitLab backend.
161: pub fn install_time_option_keys() -> Vec<String> {
162:     vec![
163:         "asset_pattern".into(),
164:         "url".into(),
165:         "version_prefix".into(),
166:         "no_app".into(),
167:     ]
168: }

171: impl Backend for UnifiedGitBackend {
172:     fn get_type(&self) -> BackendType {
173:         if self.is_gitlab() {
174:             BackendType::Gitlab
175:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
176:             BackendType::Forgejo
177:         } else {
178:             BackendType::Github
179:         }
180:     }

182:     fn ba(&self) -> &Arc<BackendArg> {
183:         &self.ba
184:     }

186:     async fn security_info(&self) -> Vec<SecurityFeature> {
187:         // Only report security features for GitHub (not GitLab yet)
188:         if self.is_gitlab() || self.is_forgejo() {
189:             return vec![];
190:         }
191: 
192:         let mut features = vec![];
193: 
194:         // Get the latest release to check for security assets
195:         let repo = self.ba.tool_name();
196:         let raw_opts = self.ba.opts();
197:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
198:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
199: 
200:         let releases = github::list_releases_from_url(api_url.as_str(), &repo)
201:             .await
202:             .unwrap_or_default();
203: 
204:         let latest_release = releases.first();
205: 
206:         // Check for checksum files in assets
207:         if let Some(release) = latest_release {
208:             let has_checksum = release.assets.iter().any(|a| {
209:                 let name = a.name.to_lowercase();
210:                 name.contains("sha256")
211:                     || name.contains("checksum")
212:                     || name.ends_with(".sha256")
213:                     || name.ends_with(".sha512")
214:             });
215:             if has_checksum {
216:                 features.push(SecurityFeature::Checksum {
217:                     algorithm: Some("sha256".to_string()),
218:                 });
219:             }
220:         }
221: 
222:         // Check for GitHub artifact Attestations (assets with .sigstore.json or .sigstore extension)
223:         if let Some(release) = latest_release {
224:             let has_attestations = release.assets.iter().any(|a| {
225:                 let name = a.name.to_lowercase();
226:                 name.ends_with(".sigstore.json") || name.ends_with(".sigstore")
227:             });
228:             if has_attestations {
229:                 features.push(SecurityFeature::GithubAttestations {
230:                     signer_workflow: None,
231:                 });
232:             }
233:         }
234: 
235:         // Check for SLSA provenance (intoto.jsonl files)
236:         if let Some(release) = latest_release {
237:             let has_slsa = release.assets.iter().any(|a| {
238:                 let name = a.name.to_lowercase();
239:                 name.contains(".intoto.jsonl")
240:                     || name.contains("provenance")
241:                     || name.ends_with(".attestation")
242:             });
243:             if has_slsa {
244:                 features.push(SecurityFeature::Slsa { level: None });
245:             }
246:         }
247: 
248:         features
249:     }
250: 
251:     fn remote_version_listing_tool_option_keys(&self) -> &'static [&'static str] {
252:         &["api_url", "version_prefix"]
253:     }
254: 
255:     async fn _list_remote_versions(&self, config: &Arc<Config>) -> Result<Vec<VersionInfo>> {
256:         let repo = self.ba.tool_name();
257:         let id = self.ba.to_string();
258:         let raw_opts = config.get_tool_opts_with_overrides(&self.ba).await?;
259:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
260:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
261:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
262: 
263:         // Derive web URL base from API URL for enterprise support
264:         let web_url_base = if self.is_gitlab() {
265:             if api_url == DEFAULT_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL {
266:                 format!("https://gitlab.com/{}", repo)
267:             } else {
268:                 // Enterprise GitLab - derive web URL from API URL
269:                 let web_url = api_url.replace("/api/v4", "");
270:                 format!("{}/{}", web_url, repo)
271:             }
272:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
273:             if api_url == DEFAULT_FORGEJO_API_BASE_URL {
274:                 format!("https://codeberg.org/{}", repo)
275:             } else {
276:                 // Enterprise Forgejo - derive web URL from API URL
277:                 let web_url = api_url.replace("/api/v1", "");
278:                 format!("{}/{}", web_url, repo)
279:             }
280:         } else if api_url == DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL {
281:             format!("https://github.com/{}", repo)
282:         } else {
283:             // Enterprise GitHub - derive web URL from API URL
284:             let web_url = api_url.replace("/api/v3", "").replace("api.", "");
285:             format!("{}/{}", web_url, repo)
286:         };
287: 
288:         // Get releases with full metadata from GitHub, GitLab, or Forgejo
289:         let raw_versions: Vec<VersionInfo> = if self.is_gitlab() {
290:             gitlab::list_releases_from_url(api_url.as_str(), &repo)
291:                 .await?
292:                 .into_iter()
293:                 .filter(|r| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| r.tag_name.starts_with(p)))
294:                 .map(|r| VersionInfo {
295:                     version: self.strip_version_prefix(&r.tag_name, &opts),
296:                     created_at: r.released_at,
297:                     release_url: Some(format!("{}/-/releases/{}", web_url_base, r.tag_name)),
298:                     ..Default::default()
299:                 })
300:                 .collect()
301:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
302:             forgejo::list_releases_including_prereleases_from_url(api_url.as_str(), &repo)
303:                 .await?
304:                 .into_iter()
305:                 .filter(|r| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| r.tag_name.starts_with(p)))
306:                 .map(|r| VersionInfo {
307:                     version: self.strip_version_prefix(&r.tag_name, &opts),
308:                     created_at: Some(r.created_at),
309:                     release_url: Some(format!("{}/releases/tag/{}", web_url_base, r.tag_name)),
310:                     prerelease: r.prerelease,
311:                     ..Default::default()
312:                 })
313:                 .collect()
314:         } else {
315:             // Always fetch the pre-release superset and stamp `prerelease` on
316:             // each entry. The shared remote-versions cache stores the superset
317:             // so flipping the `prerelease` tool option (e.g. via a project
318:             // override) is correct without invalidating the cache; the read
319:             // path filters on `prerelease` according to the current opts.
320:             github::list_releases_including_prereleases_from_url(api_url.as_str(), &repo)
321:                 .await?
322:                 .into_iter()
323:                 .filter(|r| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| r.tag_name.starts_with(p)))
324:                 .map(|r| VersionInfo {
325:                     version: self.strip_version_prefix(&r.tag_name, &opts),
326:                     created_at: Some(r.created_at),
327:                     release_url: Some(format!("{}/releases/tag/{}", web_url_base, r.tag_name)),
328:                     prerelease: r.prerelease,
329:                     ..Default::default()
330:                 })
331:                 .collect()
332:         };
333: 
334:         // Apply common validation and reverse order
335:         let versions = raw_versions
336:             .into_iter()
337:             .filter(|v| match v.version.parse::<ToolVersionType>() {
338:                 Ok(ToolVersionType::Version(_)) => true,
339:                 _ => {
340:                     warn!("Invalid version: {id}@{}", v.version);
341:                     false
342:                 }
343:             })
344:             .rev()
345:             .collect();
346: 
347:         Ok(versions)
348:     }
349: 
350:     async fn latest_stable_version(&self, config: &Arc<Config>) -> eyre::Result<Option<String>> {
351:         if Settings::get().offline() {
352:             trace!("Skipping latest stable version due to offline mode");
353:             return Ok(None);
354:         }
355: 
356:         let repo = self.ba.tool_name();
357:         let raw_opts = config.get_tool_opts_with_overrides(&self.ba).await?;
358:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
359:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
360:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
361: 
362:         // When `prerelease = true`, skip the `/releases/latest` shortcut
363:         // (which returns whichever release the repo owner marked as "Latest",
364:         // defaulting to the newest non-prerelease). Returning `None` lets the
365:         // trait's `latest_version` fall through to `latest_version_for_query`,
366:         // which resolves against the full list — now including pre-releases.
367:         if self.include_prereleases(opts.raw()) {
368:             return Ok(None);
369:         }
370: 
371:         let latest_tag = if self.is_gitlab() {
372:             // GitLab doesn't have a "latest" endpoint
373:             return Ok(None);
374:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
375:             match forgejo::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, "latest").await {
376:                 Ok(r) => Some(r.tag_name),
377:                 Err(e) => {
378:                     debug!("Failed to fetch latest Forgejo release for {repo}: {e}");
379:                     None
380:                 }
381:             }
382:         } else {
383:             match github::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, "latest").await {
384:                 Ok(r) => Some(r.tag_name),
385:                 Err(e) => {
386:                     debug!("Failed to fetch latest GitHub release for {repo}: {e}");
387:                     None
388:                 }
389:             }
390:         };
391: 
392:         let latest_version = latest_tag
393:             .filter(|tag| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| tag.starts_with(p)))
394:             .map(|tag| self.strip_version_prefix(&tag, &opts));
395: 
396:         match latest_version {
397:             Some(version) => Ok(Some(version)),
398:             None => Ok(None),
399:         }
400:     }
401: 
402:     async fn install_version_(
403:         &self,
404:         ctx: &InstallContext,
405:         mut tv: ToolVersion,
406:     ) -> Result<ToolVersion> {
407:         let repo = self.repo();
408:         let raw_opts = ctx.config.get_tool_opts_with_overrides(&self.ba).await?;
409:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
410:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
411: 
412:         // Check if URL already exists in lockfile platforms first
413:         let platform_key = self.get_platform_key();
414: 
415:         let asset = if let Some(existing_platform) = tv.lock_platforms.get(&platform_key)
416:             && existing_platform.url.is_some()
417:         {
418:             debug!(
419:                 "Using existing URL from lockfile for platform {}: {}",
420:                 platform_key,
421:                 existing_platform.url.clone().unwrap_or_default()
422:             );
423:             ReleaseAsset {
424:                 name: get_filename_from_url(existing_platform.url.as_deref().unwrap_or("")),
425:                 url: existing_platform.url.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
426:                 url_api: existing_platform.url_api.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
427:                 digest: None, // Don't use old digest from lockfile, will be fetched fresh if needed
428:             }
429:         } else {
430:             // Find the asset URL for this specific version
431:             self.resolve_asset_url(&tv, &opts, &repo, &api_url).await?
432:         };
433: 
434:         // Download and install
435:         self.download_and_install(ctx, &mut tv, &asset, &opts)
436:             .await?;
437: 
438:         Ok(tv)
439:     }

441:     async fn list_bin_paths(
442:         &self,
443:         _config: &Arc<Config>,
444:         tv: &ToolVersion,
445:     ) -> Result<Vec<std::path::PathBuf>> {
446:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
447:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
448:         let mise_bins_dir = tv.install_path().join(MISE_BINS_DIR);
449:         if opts.filter_bins().is_some() || mise_bins_dir.is_dir() {
450:             return Ok(vec![tv.runtime_path().join(MISE_BINS_DIR)]);
451:         }
452: 
453:         Ok(self
454:             .discover_bin_paths(tv)?
455:             .into_iter()
456:             .map(|path| runtime_path_for_install_path(tv, path))
457:             .collect())
458:     }
