460:     fn resolve_lockfile_options(
461:         &self,
462:         request: &ToolRequest,
463:         _target: &PlatformTarget,
464:     ) -> BTreeMap<String, String> {
465:         let raw_opts = request.options();
466:         self.options(&raw_opts).lockfile_options()
467:     }
468: 
469:     /// Resolve platform-specific lock information for cross-platform lockfile generation.
470:     /// This fetches release asset metadata including SHA256 digests from GitHub/GitLab API.
471:     async fn resolve_lock_info(
472:         &self,
473:         tv: &ToolVersion,
474:         target: &PlatformTarget,
475:     ) -> Result<PlatformInfo> {
476:         let repo = self.repo();
477:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
478:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
479:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
480: 
481:         // Resolve asset for the target platform
482:         let asset = self
483:             .resolve_asset_url_for_target(tv, &opts, &repo, &api_url, target)
484:             .await;
485: 
486:         match asset {
487:             Ok(asset) => {
488:                 // Detect provenance availability from release assets and attestation API
489:                 let (mut provenance, mut github_attestations) =
490:                     if !self.is_gitlab() && !self.is_forgejo() {
491:                         self.detect_provenance_type(
492:                             tv,
493:                             &opts,
494:                             &repo,
495:                             &api_url,
496:                             asset.digest.as_deref(),
497:                             target,
498:                         )
499:                         .await
500:                     } else {
501:                         (None, None)
502:                     };
503: 
504:                 // For the current platform, verify provenance cryptographically at lock time.
505:                 // This ensures the lockfile's provenance entry is backed by actual verification,
506:                 // not just an API query. Cross-platform entries remain detection-only.
507:                 if provenance.is_some() && target.is_current() {
508:                     match self
509:                         .verify_provenance_at_lock_time(tv, &opts, &repo, &api_url, &asset)
510:                         .await
511:                     {
512:                         Ok((verified, gh_status)) => {
513:                             provenance = verified;
514:                             github_attestations = gh_status;
515:                         }
516:                         Err(e) => {
517:                             // Clear provenance so install-time verification will run.
518:                             warn!(
519:                                 "lock-time provenance verification failed for {}, \
520:                                  will be verified at install time: {e}",
521:                                 self.ba.full()
522:                             );
523:                             provenance = None;
524:                         }
525:                     }
526:                 }
527:                 if provenance.is_some() {
528:                     github_attestations = None;
529:                 }
530: 
531:                 Ok(PlatformInfo {
532:                     url: Some(asset.url),
533:                     url_api: Some(asset.url_api),
534:                     checksum: asset.digest,
535:                     provenance,
536:                     github_attestations,
537:                     ..Default::default()
538:                 })
539:             }
540:             Err(e) => {
541:                 debug!(
542:                     "Failed to resolve asset for {} on {}: {}",
543:                     self.ba.full(),
544:                     target.to_key(),
545:                     e
546:                 );
547:                 Ok(PlatformInfo::default())
548:             }
549:         }
550:     }
551: }
552: 
553: impl UnifiedGitBackend {
554:     pub fn from_arg(ba: BackendArg) -> Self {
555:         Self { ba: Arc::new(ba) }
556:     }
557: 
558:     fn options<'a>(&self, raw: &'a ToolVersionOptions) -> GitBackendOptions<'a> {
559:         GitBackendOptions::new(raw, self.default_api_url())
560:     }
561: 
562:     fn default_api_url(&self) -> &'static str {
563:         if self.is_gitlab() {
564:             DEFAULT_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL
565:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
566:             DEFAULT_FORGEJO_API_BASE_URL
567:         } else {
568:             DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL
569:         }
570:     }
571: 
572:     /// Detect what provenance type is available for a release by checking its assets
573:     /// and querying the GitHub attestation API.
574:     async fn detect_provenance_type(
575:         &self,
576:         tv: &ToolVersion,
577:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
578:         repo: &str,
579:         api_url: &str,
580:         asset_digest: Option<&str>,
581:         target: &PlatformTarget,
582:     ) -> (Option<ProvenanceType>, Option<GithubAttestationsStatus>) {
583:         let settings = Settings::get();
584:         let version = &tv.version;
585:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
586:         let mut github_attestations = None;
587: 
588:         let release =
589:             try_with_v_prefix_and_repo(version, version_prefix, Some(repo), |candidate| {
590:                 let api_url = api_url.to_string();
591:                 let repo = repo.to_string();
592:                 async move { github::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, &candidate).await }
593:             })
594:             .await
595:             .ok();
596:         let Some(release) = release else {
597:             return (None, None);
598:         };
599: 
600:         // Check github-attestations first (higher priority, matching install verification order)
601:         // Uses the asset digest from the GitHub API to query attestations without downloading
602:         if settings.github_attestations
603:             && settings.github.github_attestations
604:             && attestations_supported(api_url)
605:             && let Some(digest) = asset_digest
606:         {
607:             let parts: Vec<&str> = repo.split('/').collect();
608:             if parts.len() == 2 {
609:                 let (owner, repo_name) = (parts[0], parts[1]);
610:                 match crate::github::sigstore::detect_attestations(
611:                     owner, repo_name, api_url, digest,
612:                 )
613:                 .await
614:                 {
615:                     Ok(true) => return (Some(ProvenanceType::GithubAttestations), None),
616:                     Ok(false) => {
617:                         github_attestations = Some(GithubAttestationsStatus::Unavailable);
618:                     }
619:                     Err(crate::github::sigstore::DetectError::SourceCreation(e)) => {
620:                         warn!(
621:                             "Failed to create GitHub attestation source for {owner}/{repo_name}: {e}. \
622:                              Lockfile may not record github-attestations provenance."
623:                         );
624:                     }
625:                     Err(crate::github::sigstore::DetectError::Fetch(e)) => {
626:                         warn!(
627:                             "GitHub attestation API query failed for {owner}/{repo_name}: {e}. \
628:                              Lockfile may not record github-attestations provenance."
629:                         );
630:                     }
631:                 }
632:             }
633:         }
634: 
635:         // Check for SLSA provenance from release assets using the same platform-aware
636:         // picker as install-time verification. This ensures we only record SLSA provenance
637:         // when a matching provenance file exists for the target platform.
638:         if settings.slsa && settings.github.slsa {
639:             let asset_names: Vec<String> = release.assets.iter().map(|a| a.name.clone()).collect();
640:             let picker = AssetPicker::with_libc(
641:                 target.os_name().to_string(),
642:                 target.arch_name().to_string(),
643:                 target.qualifier().map(|s| s.to_string()),
644:             );
645:             if let Some(provenance_name) = picker.pick_best_provenance(&asset_names) {
646:                 let url = release
647:                     .assets
648:                     .iter()
649:                     .find(|a| a.name == provenance_name)
650:                     .map(|a| a.browser_download_url.clone());
651:                 return (Some(ProvenanceType::Slsa { url }), None);
652:             }
653:         }
654: 
655:         (None, github_attestations)
656:     }
657: 
658:     /// Verify provenance at lock time by downloading the artifact to a temp directory
659:     /// and running cryptographic verification. Only called for the current platform
660:     /// during `mise lock`.
661:     async fn verify_provenance_at_lock_time(
662:         &self,
663:         tv: &ToolVersion,
664:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
665:         repo: &str,
666:         api_url: &str,
667:         asset: &ReleaseAsset,
668:     ) -> Result<(Option<ProvenanceType>, Option<GithubAttestationsStatus>)> {
669:         let tmp_dir = tempfile::tempdir()?;
670:         let filename = get_filename_from_url(&asset.url);
671:         let artifact_path = tmp_dir.path().join(&filename);
672: 
673:         info!(
674:             "downloading artifact for lock-time provenance verification: {}",
675:             filename
676:         );
677: 
678:         // Use the API URL with appropriate headers for downloading
679:         let download_url = if self.is_gitlab() {
680:             asset.url.clone()
681:         } else {
682:             asset.url_api.clone()
683:         };
684:         let headers = if self.is_gitlab() {
685:             gitlab::get_headers(&download_url)
686:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
687:             forgejo::get_headers(&download_url)
688:         } else {
689:             github::get_headers(&download_url)
690:         };
691:         HTTP.download_file_with_headers(&download_url, &artifact_path, &headers, None)
692:             .await?;
693: 
694:         let settings = Settings::get();
695:         let mut github_attestations = None;
696: 
697:         // Try GitHub artifact attestations first (highest priority)
698:         if settings.github_attestations
699:             && settings.github.github_attestations
700:             && attestations_supported(api_url)
701:         {
702:             let parts: Vec<&str> = repo.split('/').collect();
703:             if parts.len() == 2 {
704:                 let (owner, repo_name) = (parts[0], parts[1]);
705:                 match crate::github::sigstore::verify_attestation(
706:                     &artifact_path,
707:                     owner,
708:                     repo_name,
709:                     None,
710:                     Some(api_url),
711:                 )
712:                 .await
713:                 {
714:                     Ok(true) => {
715:                         debug!("lock-time GitHub attestations verified for {}", repo);
716:                         return Ok((Some(ProvenanceType::GithubAttestations), None));
717:                     }
718:                     Ok(false) => {
719:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!(
720:                             "GitHub artifact attestations verification returned false"
721:                         ));
722:                     }
723:                     Err(crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::NoAttestations) => {
724:                         github_attestations = Some(GithubAttestationsStatus::Unavailable);
725:                         debug!("no GitHub attestations found at lock time, trying SLSA");
726:                     }
727:                     Err(e) => {
728:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!(
729:                             "GitHub artifact attestations verification failed: {e}"
730:                         ));
731:                     }
732:                 }
733:             }
734:         }
735: 
736:         // Fall back to SLSA provenance
737:         if settings.slsa && settings.github.slsa {
738:             let version = &tv.version;
739:             let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
740:             let release =
741:                 try_with_v_prefix_and_repo(version, version_prefix, Some(repo), |candidate| {
742:                     let api_url = api_url.to_string();
743:                     let repo = repo.to_string();
744:                     async move { github::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, &candidate).await }
745:                 })
746:                 .await?;
747: 
748:             let asset_names: Vec<String> = release.assets.iter().map(|a| a.name.clone()).collect();
749:             let current_platform = PlatformTarget::from_current();
750:             let picker = AssetPicker::with_libc(
751:                 current_platform.os_name().to_string(),
752:                 current_platform.arch_name().to_string(),
753:                 current_platform.qualifier().map(|s| s.to_string()),
754:             );
755: 
756:             if let Some(provenance_name) = picker.pick_best_provenance(&asset_names) {
757:                 let provenance_asset = release
758:                     .assets
759:                     .iter()
760:                     .find(|a| a.name == provenance_name)
761:                     .expect("provenance asset should exist since we found its name");
762: 
763:                 let provenance_path = tmp_dir.path().join(&provenance_asset.name);
764:                 HTTP.download_file(
765:                     &provenance_asset.browser_download_url,
766:                     &provenance_path,
767:                     None,
768:                 )
769:                 .await?;
770: 
771:                 let provenance_url = provenance_asset.browser_download_url.clone();
772:                 match crate::github::sigstore::verify_slsa_provenance(
773:                     &artifact_path,
774:                     &provenance_path,
775:                     1u8,
776:                 )
777:                 .await
778:                 {
779:                     Ok(true) => {
780:                         debug!("lock-time SLSA provenance verified for {}", repo);
781:                         return Ok((
782:                             Some(ProvenanceType::Slsa {
783:                                 url: Some(provenance_url),
784:                             }),
785:                             None,
786:                         ));
787:                     }
788:                     Ok(false) => {
789:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!("SLSA provenance verification failed"));
790:                     }
791:                     Err(e) => {
792:                         if is_slsa_format_issue(&e) {
793:                             debug!("SLSA provenance file not in verifiable format: {e}");
794:                         } else {
795:                             return Err(eyre::eyre!("SLSA verification error: {e}"));
796:                         }
797:                     }
798:                 }
799:             }
800:         }
801: 
802:         if github_attestations.is_some() {
803:             Ok((None, github_attestations))
804:         } else {
805:             Err(eyre::eyre!(
806:                 "provenance was detected but could not be verified at lock time"
807:             ))
808:         }
809:     }
810: 
811:     fn is_gitlab(&self) -> bool {
812:         self.ba.backend_type() == BackendType::Gitlab
813:     }
814: 
815:     fn is_forgejo(&self) -> bool {
816:         self.ba.backend_type() == BackendType::Forgejo
817:     }
818: 
819:     fn repo(&self) -> String {
820:         // Use tool_name() method to properly resolve aliases
821:         // This ensures that when an alias like "test-edit = github:microsoft/edit" is used,
822:         // the repository name is correctly extracted as "microsoft/edit"
823:         self.ba.tool_name()
824:     }

826:     // Helper to format asset names for error messages
827:     fn format_asset_list<'a, I>(assets: I) -> String
828:     where
829:         I: Iterator<Item = &'a String>,
830:     {
831:         assets.cloned().collect::<Vec<_>>().join(", ")
832:     }

834:     /// Downloads and installs the asset
835:     async fn download_and_install(
836:         &self,
837:         ctx: &InstallContext,
838:         tv: &mut ToolVersion,
839:         asset: &ReleaseAsset,
840:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
841:     ) -> Result<()> {
842:         let filename = asset.name.clone();
843:         let file_path = tv.download_path().join(&filename);
844: 
845:         // Check if we'll verify checksum
846:         let has_checksum = opts.checksum().is_some();
847: 
848:         // Store the asset URL and digest (if available) in the tool version
849:         let platform_key = self.get_platform_key();
850:         let platform_info = tv.lock_platforms.entry(platform_key).or_default();
851:         platform_info.url = Some(asset.url.clone());
852:         platform_info.url_api = Some(asset.url_api.clone());
853:         if let Some(digest) = &asset.digest {
854:             debug!("using GitHub API digest for checksum verification");
855:             platform_info.checksum = Some(digest.clone());
856:         }
857: 
858:         let url = match asset.url_api.starts_with(DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL)
859:             || asset.url_api.starts_with(DEFAULT_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL)
860:             || asset.url_api.starts_with(DEFAULT_FORGEJO_API_BASE_URL)
861:         {
862:             // check if url is reachable, 404 might indicate a private repo or asset.
863:             // This is needed, because private repos and assets cannot be downloaded
864:             // via browser url, therefore a fallback to api_url is needed in such cases.
865:             // Also check Content-Type - if it's text/html, we got a login page (private repo).
866:             true => match HTTP.head(asset.url.clone()).await {
867:                 Ok(resp) => {
868:                     let content_type = resp
869:                         .headers()
870:                         .get("content-type")
871:                         .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
872:                         .unwrap_or("");
873:                     if content_type.contains("text/html") {
874:                         debug!("Browser URL returned HTML (likely auth page), using API URL");
875:                         asset.url_api.clone()
876:                     } else {
877:                         asset.url.clone()
878:                     }
879:                 }
880:                 Err(_) => asset.url_api.clone(),
881:             },
882: 
883:             // Custom API URLs usually imply that a custom GitHub/GitLab instance is used.
884:             // Often times such instances do not allow browser URL downloads, e.g. due to
885:             // upstream company SSOs. Therefore, using the api_url for downloading is the safer approach.
886:             false => {
887:                 debug!(
888:                     "Since the tool resides on a custom GitHub/GitLab API ({:?}), the asset download will be performed using the given API instead of browser URL download",
889:                     asset.url_api
890:                 );
891:                 asset.url_api.clone()
892:             }
893:         };
894: 
895:         let headers = if self.is_gitlab() {
896:             gitlab::get_headers(&url)
897:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
898:             forgejo::get_headers(&url)
899:         } else {
900:             github::get_headers(&url)
901:         };
902: 
903:         ctx.pr.set_message(format!("download {filename}"));
904:         HTTP.download_file_with_headers(url, &file_path, &headers, Some(ctx.pr.as_ref()))
905:             .await?;
906: 
907:         // Verify and install
908:         ctx.pr.next_operation();
909:         if has_checksum {
910:             verify_artifact(tv, &file_path, opts.raw(), Some(ctx.pr.as_ref()))?;
911:         }
912: 
913:         // Check before verify_checksum, which may generate a new checksum from the
914:         // downloaded file. We only want to skip provenance when the lockfile already
915:         // had integrity data before this install.
916:         let platform_key = self.get_platform_key();
917:         let has_lockfile_integrity = tv
918:             .lock_platforms
919:             .get(&platform_key)
920:             .is_some_and(PlatformInfo::has_checksum_and_verified_provenance);
921: 
922:         self.verify_checksum(ctx, tv, &file_path)?;
923: 
924:         let settings = Settings::get();
925:         let force_verify = settings.force_provenance_verify();
926:         if has_lockfile_integrity && !force_verify {
927:             // Still check that the recorded provenance type's setting is enabled —
928:             // disabling a verification setting with a provenance-bearing lockfile is a downgrade.
929:             self.ensure_provenance_setting_enabled(tv, &platform_key)?;
930:         } else {
931:             let (provenance_result, github_attestations) = self
932:                 .verify_attestations_or_slsa(ctx, tv, &file_path)
933:                 .await?;
934: 
935:             // Record provenance verification result in lock_platforms
936:             if provenance_result.is_some() || github_attestations.is_some() {
937:                 let platform_info = tv.lock_platforms.entry(platform_key).or_default();
938:                 platform_info.provenance = provenance_result;
939:                 platform_info.github_attestations = github_attestations;
940:             }
941:         }
942: 
943:         ctx.pr.next_operation();
944:         install_artifact(tv, &file_path, opts.raw(), Some(ctx.pr.as_ref()))?;
945: 
946:         if let Some(bins) = opts.filter_bins() {
947:             self.create_symlink_bin_dir(tv, bins)?;
948:         }
949: 
950:         Ok(())
951:     }
952: 
953:     /// Discovers bin paths in the installation directory
954:     fn discover_bin_paths(&self, tv: &ToolVersion) -> Result<Vec<std::path::PathBuf>> {
955:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
956:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
957:         if let Some(bin_path_template) = opts.bin_path() {
958:             let bin_path = template_string(&bin_path_template, tv);
959:             return Ok(vec![tv.install_path().join(&bin_path)]);
960:         }
961: 
962:         let bin_path = tv.install_path().join("bin");
963:         if bin_path.exists() {
964:             return Ok(vec![bin_path]);
965:         }
966: 
967:         // Check for macOS .app bundle structure at root (happens when auto-strip removed .app wrapper)
968:         // Look for Contents/MacOS/ which indicates a stripped .app bundle
969:         let contents_macos = tv.install_path().join("Contents").join("MacOS");
970:         if contents_macos.is_dir() {
971:             return Ok(vec![contents_macos]);
972:         }
973: 
974:         // Check if the root directory contains an executable file
975:         // If so, use the root directory as a bin path
976:         if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(tv.install_path()) {
977:             for entry in entries.flatten() {
978:                 let path = entry.path();
979:                 if path.is_file() && file::is_executable(&path) {
980:                     return Ok(vec![tv.install_path()]);
981:                 }
982:             }
983:         }
984: 
985:         // Look for bin directory or executables in subdirectories (for extracted archives)
986:         let mut paths = Vec::new();
987:         if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(tv.install_path()) {
988:             for entry in entries.flatten() {
989:                 let path = entry.path();
990:                 if path.is_dir() {
991:                     // Check for macOS .app bundles (e.g., SwiftFormat.app/Contents/MacOS/)
992:                     let path_str = path.file_name().unwrap_or_default().to_string_lossy();
993:                     if path_str.ends_with(".app") {
994:                         let macos_dir = path.join("Contents").join("MacOS");
995:                         if macos_dir.is_dir() {
996:                             paths.push(macos_dir);
997:                             continue;
998:                         }
999:                     }
1000:                     // Check for {subdir}/bin
1001:                     let sub_bin_path = path.join("bin");
1002:                     if sub_bin_path.exists() {
1003:                         paths.push(sub_bin_path);
1004:                     } else {
1005:                         // Check for executables directly in subdir (e.g., tusd_darwin_arm64/tusd)
1006:                         if let Ok(sub_entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&path) {
1007:                             for sub_entry in sub_entries.flatten() {
1008:                                 let sub_path = sub_entry.path();
1009:                                 if sub_path.is_file() && file::is_executable(&sub_path) {
1010:                                     paths.push(path.clone());
1011:                                     break;
1012:                                 }
1013:                             }
1014:                         }
1015:                     }
1016:                 }
1017:             }
1018:         }
1019: 
1020:         if paths.is_empty() {
1021:             Ok(vec![tv.install_path()])
1022:         } else {
1023:             Ok(paths)
1024:         }
1025:     }

1027:     /// Resolves the asset URL using either explicit patterns or auto-detection.
1028:     /// Delegates to resolve_asset_url_for_target with the current platform.
1029:     async fn resolve_asset_url(
1030:         &self,
1031:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1032:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
1033:         repo: &str,
1034:         api_url: &str,
1035:     ) -> Result<ReleaseAsset> {
1036:         let current_platform = PlatformTarget::from_current();
1037:         self.resolve_asset_url_for_target(tv, opts, repo, api_url, &current_platform)
1038:             .await
1039:     }
1040: 
1041:     /// Resolves asset URL for a specific target platform (for cross-platform lockfile generation)
1042:     async fn resolve_asset_url_for_target(
1043:         &self,
1044:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1045:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
1046:         repo: &str,
1047:         api_url: &str,
1048:         target: &PlatformTarget,
1049:     ) -> Result<ReleaseAsset> {
1050:         // Check for direct platform-specific URLs first
1051:         if let Some(direct_url) = opts.direct_url_for_target(target) {
1052:             return Ok(ReleaseAsset {
1053:                 name: get_filename_from_url(&direct_url),
1054:                 url: direct_url.clone(),
1055:                 url_api: direct_url.clone(),
1056:                 digest: None, // Direct URLs don't have API digest
1057:             });
1058:         }
1059: 
1060:         let version = &tv.version;
1061:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
1062:         if self.is_gitlab() {
1063:             try_with_v_prefix(version, version_prefix, |candidate| async move {
1064:                 self.resolve_gitlab_asset_url_for_target(
1065:                     tv, opts, repo, api_url, &candidate, target,
1066:                 )
1067:                 .await
1068:             })
1069:             .await
1070:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
1071:             try_with_v_prefix(version, version_prefix, |candidate| async move {
1072:                 self.resolve_forgejo_asset_url_for_target(
1073:                     tv, opts, repo, api_url, &candidate, target,
1074:                 )
1075:                 .await
1076:             })
1077:             .await
1078:         } else {
1079:             // Pass full repo for trying reponame@version formats
1080:             try_with_v_prefix_and_repo(
1081:                 version,
1082:                 version_prefix,
1083:                 Some(repo),
1084:                 |candidate| async move {
1085:                     self.resolve_github_asset_url_for_target(
1086:                         tv, opts, repo, api_url, &candidate, target,
1087:                     )
1088:                     .await
1089:                 },
1090:             )
1091:             .await
1092:         }
1093:     }
1094: 
1095:     /// Resolves GitHub asset URL for a specific target platform
1096:     async fn resolve_github_asset_url_for_target(
1097:         &self,
1098:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1099:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
1100:         repo: &str,
1101:         api_url: &str,
1102:         version: &str,
1103:         target: &PlatformTarget,
1104:     ) -> Result<ReleaseAsset> {
1105:         let release = github::get_release_for_url(api_url, repo, version).await?;
1106:         let available_assets: Vec<String> = release.assets.iter().map(|a| a.name.clone()).collect();
1107: 
1108:         // Build asset list with URLs for checksum fetching
1109:         let assets_with_urls: Vec<Asset> = release
1110:             .assets
1111:             .iter()
1112:             .map(|a| Asset::new(&a.name, &a.browser_download_url))
1113:             .collect();
1114: 
1115:         // Try explicit pattern first
1116:         if let Some(pattern) = opts.asset_pattern_for_target(target) {
1117:             // Template the pattern for the target platform
1118:             let templated_pattern = template_string_for_target(&pattern, tv, target);
1119: 
1120:             let asset = release
1121:                 .assets
1122:                 .into_iter()
1123:                 .find(|a| self.matches_pattern(&a.name, &templated_pattern))
1124:                 .ok_or_else(|| {
1125:                     eyre::eyre!(
1126:                         "No matching asset found for pattern: {}\nAvailable assets: {}",
1127:                         templated_pattern,
1128:                         Self::format_asset_list(available_assets.iter())
1129:                     )
1130:                 })?;
1131: 
1132:             // Try to get checksum from API digest or fetch from release assets
1133:             let digest = if asset.digest.is_some() {
1134:                 asset.digest
1135:             } else {
1136:                 self.try_fetch_checksum_from_assets(&assets_with_urls, &asset.name)
1137:                     .await
1138:             };
1139: 
1140:             return Ok(ReleaseAsset {
1141:                 name: asset.name,
1142:                 url: asset.browser_download_url,
1143:                 url_api: asset.url,
1144:                 digest,
1145:             });
1146:         }
1147: 
1148:         // Fall back to auto-detection for target platform
1149:         let asset_name = asset_matcher::AssetMatcher::new()
1150:             .for_target(target)
1151:             .with_no_app(opts.no_app_for_target(target))
1152:             .pick_from(&available_assets)?
1153:             .name;
1154:         let asset = self
1155:             .find_asset_case_insensitive(&release.assets, &asset_name, |a| &a.name)
1156:             .ok_or_else(|| {
1157:                 eyre::eyre!(
1158:                     "Auto-detected asset not found: {}\nAvailable assets: {}",
1159:                     asset_name,
1160:                     Self::format_asset_list(available_assets.iter())
1161:                 )
1162:             })?;
1163: 
1164:         // Try to get checksum from API digest or fetch from release assets
1165:         let digest = if asset.digest.is_some() {
1166:             asset.digest.clone()
1167:         } else {
1168:             self.try_fetch_checksum_from_assets(&assets_with_urls, &asset.name)
1169:                 .await
1170:         };
1171: 
1172:         Ok(ReleaseAsset {
1173:             name: asset.name.clone(),
1174:             url: asset.browser_download_url.clone(),
1175:             url_api: asset.url.clone(),
1176:             digest,
1177:         })
1178:     }
1179: 
1180:     /// Resolves GitLab asset URL for a specific target platform
1181:     async fn resolve_gitlab_asset_url_for_target(
1182:         &self,
1183:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1184:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
1185:         repo: &str,
1186:         api_url: &str,
1187:         version: &str,
1188:         target: &PlatformTarget,
1189:     ) -> Result<ReleaseAsset> {
1190:         let release = gitlab::get_release_for_url(api_url, repo, version).await?;
1191:         let available_assets: Vec<String> = release
1192:             .assets
1193:             .links
1194:             .iter()
1195:             .map(|a| a.name.clone())
1196:             .collect();
1197: 
1198:         // Build asset list with URLs for checksum fetching
1199:         let assets_with_urls: Vec<Asset> = release
1200:             .assets
1201:             .links
1202:             .iter()
1203:             .map(|a| Asset::new(&a.name, &a.direct_asset_url))
1204:             .collect();
1205: 
1206:         // Try explicit pattern first
1207:         if let Some(pattern) = opts.asset_pattern_for_target(target) {
1208:             // Template the pattern for the target platform
1209:             let templated_pattern = template_string_for_target(&pattern, tv, target);
1210: 
1211:             let asset = release
1212:                 .assets
1213:                 .links
1214:                 .into_iter()
1215:                 .find(|a| self.matches_pattern(&a.name, &templated_pattern))
1216:                 .ok_or_else(|| {
1217:                     eyre::eyre!(
1218:                         "No matching asset found for pattern: {}\nAvailable assets: {}",
1219:                         templated_pattern,
1220:                         Self::format_asset_list(available_assets.iter())
1221:                     )
1222:                 })?;
1223: 
1224:             // GitLab doesn't provide digests, so try fetching from release assets
1225:             let digest = self
1226:                 .try_fetch_checksum_from_assets(&assets_with_urls, &asset.name)
1227:                 .await;
1228: 
1229:             return Ok(ReleaseAsset {
1230:                 name: asset.name,
1231:                 url: asset.direct_asset_url.clone(),
1232:                 url_api: asset.url,
1233:                 digest,
1234:             });
1235:         }
1236: 
1237:         // Fall back to auto-detection for target platform
1238:         let asset_name = asset_matcher::AssetMatcher::new()
1239:             .for_target(target)
1240:             .with_no_app(opts.no_app_for_target(target))
1241:             .pick_from(&available_assets)?
1242:             .name;
1243:         let asset = self
1244:             .find_asset_case_insensitive(&release.assets.links, &asset_name, |a| &a.name)
1245:             .ok_or_else(|| {
1246:                 eyre::eyre!(
1247:                     "Auto-detected asset not found: {}\nAvailable assets: {}",
1248:                     asset_name,
1249:                     Self::format_asset_list(available_assets.iter())
1250:                 )
1251:             })?;
1252: 
1253:         // GitLab doesn't provide digests, so try fetching from release assets
1254:         let digest = self
1255:             .try_fetch_checksum_from_assets(&assets_with_urls, &asset.name)
1256:             .await;
1257: 
1258:         Ok(ReleaseAsset {
1259:             name: asset.name.clone(),
1260:             url: asset.direct_asset_url.clone(),
1261:             url_api: asset.url.clone(),
1262:             digest,
1263:         })
1264:     }
1265: 
1266:     /// Resolves Forgejo asset URL for a specific target platform
1267:     async fn resolve_forgejo_asset_url_for_target(
1268:         &self,
1269:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1270:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
1271:         repo: &str,
1272:         api_url: &str,
1273:         version: &str,
1274:         target: &PlatformTarget,
1275:     ) -> Result<ReleaseAsset> {
1276:         let release = forgejo::get_release_for_url(api_url, repo, version).await?;
1277:         let available_assets: Vec<String> = release.assets.iter().map(|a| a.name.clone()).collect();
1278: 
1279:         // Build asset list with URLs for checksum fetching
1280:         let assets_with_urls: Vec<Asset> = release
1281:             .assets
1282:             .iter()
1283:             .map(|a| Asset::new(&a.name, &a.browser_download_url))
1284:             .collect();
1285: 
1286:         // Helper to build API attachment URL
1287:         let asset_url_api = |asset_uuid: &str| {
1288:             format!(
1289:                 "{}/attachments/{}",
1290:                 api_url.replace("/api/v1", ""),
1291:                 asset_uuid
1292:             )
1293:         };
1294: 
1295:         // Try explicit pattern first
1296:         if let Some(pattern) = opts.asset_pattern_for_target(target) {
1297:             // Template the pattern for the target platform
1298:             let templated_pattern = template_string_for_target(&pattern, tv, target);
1299: 
1300:             let asset = release
1301:                 .assets
1302:                 .into_iter()
1303:                 .find(|a| self.matches_pattern(&a.name, &templated_pattern))

part.text:
Now I have a good understanding. Let me think about the design.

## The scenario

GitHub artifact attestations are signed over a digest (sha256) of an artifact. The typical flow:
1. Download file F
2. Compute digest D = sha256(F)
3. Query GitHub attestation API for D
4. Verify the bundle's signature chain and that the statement's subject digest matches D

The "per-file-in-archive" scenario: some release pipelines sign the individual *contents* of an archive (e.g. each binary), not the archive blob itself. So if you download `tool-1.0.tar.gz`, querying GitHub's API with `sha256(tool-1.0.tar.gz)` returns no attestations, but querying with `sha256(tool-1.0-binary)` (an entry inside the archive) does return an attestation.

## How to "accept safely"

The security guarantee we want: every executable file that ends up *on the user's PATH* must be covered by a verified attestation. The fallback is: if archive-level verification fails with `NoAttestations`, extract the archive to a temp dir, and verify each *file inside* it. If every relevant inner file has a verified attestation, we accept. Otherwise fail closed.

But this is delicate:
- We don't want to require attestations for metadata files (README, LICENSE, etc.)
- We don't want to be permissive: if an attacker could substitute the archive's contents, they could include *some* attested files but smuggle an unattested malicious binary.

The safest approach: require *every file inside the archive* to be attested. But that's too strict — many archives contain unattested license/readme files.

Better safer approach: require that **every executable file** in the archive (or every file matching the type we'd install) is attested. But still risky — what defines "executable"?

Let me think about this from the actual mise flow:
- mise downloads an archive (tar/zip)
- extracts it to install dir
- finds bin paths

So the files that end up "on PATH" are executables. So a reasonable policy: if archive-level verification fails, extract to temp, and verify that **every executable file inside** is attested. If even one executable is missing an attestation, fail closed.

But wait, the question says: "Some GitHub releases attest each file inside an archive rather than the archive blob itself."

I think the design intent is:
1. **Preserve existing archive-level verification** (still try the archive blob first).
2. **If archive-level returns NoAttestations** (the API says there's no attestation for the archive's digest), **fall back to per-file verification**:
   - Extract the archive to a temp dir.
   - For every file inside, attempt attestation verification.
   - Accept only if **every** file is covered by a verified attestation (fail closed).
   - Or maybe only check "interesting" files (executables)? 

Hmm. Let me think about the trust boundary. The strictest interpretation of "fail closed" is: every file must be attested. The more pragmatic one: only verify a specific subset.

Actually, I think the safest and simplest is: **every regular file in the archive must verify**. That's maximally strict. But it might reject legitimate archives that contain unattested metadata files... Hmm.

Actually wait. The way GitHub attestations work: a single digest is signed. If a release attests "each file inside", then each file has its own attestation. So the question is: which files do we check?

Let me look at the actual scenario more carefully. The task says: "Some GitHub releases attest each file inside an archive rather than the archive blob itself."

So the publisher:
1. Builds N binaries
2. Attasts each binary
3. Tarballs them together with metadata
4. Uploads the tarball to a GitHub release

When we download and try archive-level verification, we get NoAttestations. We then want to fall back to per-file verification.

The trust boundary question: what counts as "covered"?
- Option A: every file must be attested (strictest, may reject legit archives)
- Option B: only executable files must be attested
- Option C: only files we're going to install/use

I think the safest, most defensible answer is **Option A: every regular file in the archive**. The reasoning: if we accept based on a subset, an attacker who controls the archive contents (but not the attestation signing key) could substitute unattested malicious files for the unattested-but-present files in a legit release. So we must require every file to be attested.

But wait, this would be too strict for archives that bundle unattested READMEs, licenses, etc. Hmm.

Actually, let me re-read the task: "the fallback must fail closed." and "do not guess toward permissiveness."

So the safest interpretation is: **every file in the archive must verify**. If even one file is missing an attestation, fail.

Let me ask the user about this trust-boundary decision. Actually, the task says "You may use `ask_user` for consequential trust-boundary or scope questions; do not guess toward permissiveness." So I should ask.

Let me first understand a bit more about how mise extracts archives to understand what "each file inside" means.

21: /// Fetches a checksum for a specific file from a SHASUMS256.txt-style file.
22: /// Uses cached HTTP requests since the same SHASUMS file is fetched for all platforms.
23: ///
24: /// # Arguments
25: /// * `shasums_url` - URL to the SHASUMS256.txt file
26: /// * `filename` - The filename to look up in the SHASUMS file
27: ///
28: /// # Returns
29: /// * `Some("sha256:<hash>")` if found
30: /// * `None` if the SHASUMS file couldn't be fetched or filename not found
31: pub async fn fetch_checksum_from_shasums(shasums_url: &str, filename: &str) -> Option<String> {
32:     match HTTP.get_text_cached(shasums_url).await {
33:         Ok(shasums_content) => {
34:             let shasums = hash::parse_shasums(&shasums_content);
35:             shasums.get(filename).map(|h| format!("sha256:{h}"))
36:         }
37:         Err(e) => {
38:             debug!("Failed to fetch SHASUMS from {}: {e}", shasums_url);
39:             None
40:         }
41:     }
42: }
43: 
44: /// Fetches a checksum from an individual checksum file (e.g., file.tar.gz.sha256).
45: /// The checksum file should contain just the hash, optionally followed by filename.
46: ///
47: /// # Arguments
48: /// * `checksum_url` - URL to the checksum file (e.g., `https://example.com/file.tar.gz.sha256`)
49: /// * `algo` - The algorithm name to prefix (e.g., "sha256")
50: ///
51: /// # Returns
52: /// * `Some("<algo>:<hash>")` if found
53: /// * `None` if the checksum file couldn't be fetched
54: pub async fn fetch_checksum_from_file(checksum_url: &str, algo: &str) -> Option<String> {
55:     match HTTP.get_text(checksum_url).await {
56:         Ok(content) => {
57:             // Format is typically "<hash>  <filename>" or just "<hash>"
58:             content
59:                 .split_whitespace()
60:                 .next()
61:                 .map(|h| format!("{algo}:{}", h.trim()))
62:         }
63:         Err(e) => {
64:             debug!("Failed to fetch checksum from {}: {e}", checksum_url);
65:             None
66:         }
67:     }
68: }

83: pub trait VerifiableError: Sized + Send + Sync + 'static {
84:     fn is_not_found(&self) -> bool;
85:     fn into_eyre(self) -> eyre::Report;
86: }
87: 
88: impl VerifiableError for eyre::Report {
89:     fn is_not_found(&self) -> bool {
90:         if self.to_string().contains("404") {
91:             return true;
92:         }
93:         self.chain().any(|cause| {
94:             if let Some(err) = cause.downcast_ref::<reqwest::Error>() {
95:                 err.status() == Some(reqwest::StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)
96:             } else {
97:                 false
98:             }
99:         })
100:     }
101: 
102:     fn into_eyre(self) -> eyre::Report {
103:         self
104:     }
105: }

107: /// Helper to try both prefixed and non-prefixed tags for a resolver function
108: pub async fn try_with_v_prefix<F, Fut, T, E>(
109:     version: &str,
110:     version_prefix: Option<&str>,
111:     resolver: F,
112: ) -> Result<T>
113: where
114:     F: Fn(String) -> Fut,
115:     Fut: Future<Output = std::result::Result<T, E>>,
116:     E: VerifiableError,
117: {
118:     try_with_v_prefix_and_repo(version, version_prefix, None, resolver).await
119: }
120: 
121: /// Helper to try various tag formats for a resolver function
122: /// Tries version_prefix (if set), v prefix, and optionally repo@version formats
123: pub async fn try_with_v_prefix_and_repo<F, Fut, T, E>(
124:     version: &str,
125:     version_prefix: Option<&str>,
126:     repo: Option<&str>,
127:     resolver: F,
128: ) -> Result<T>
129: where
130:     F: Fn(String) -> Fut,
131:     Fut: Future<Output = std::result::Result<T, E>>,
132:     E: VerifiableError,
133: {
134:     let mut errors = vec![];
135: 
136:     // Generate candidates based on version prefix configuration
137:     let mut candidates = if let Some(prefix) = version_prefix {
138:         // If a custom prefix is configured, try both prefixed and non-prefixed versions
139:         if version.starts_with(prefix) {
140:             vec![
141:                 version.to_string(),
142:                 version.trim_start_matches(prefix).to_string(),
143:             ]
144:         } else {
145:             vec![format!("{}{}", prefix, version), version.to_string()]
146:         }
147:     } else if version == "latest" {
148:         vec![version.to_string()]
149:     } else if version.starts_with('v') {
150:         vec![
151:             version.to_string(),
152:             version.trim_start_matches('v').to_string(),
153:         ]
154:     } else {
155:         vec![format!("v{version}"), version.to_string()]
156:     };
157: 
158:     // Also try repo@version formats (e.g., tectonic@0.15.0) when no prefix is configured
159:     // Try both the repo short name and full repo name
160:     // Skip this for "latest" since it's a special keyword, not an actual tag
161:     if version_prefix.is_none()
162:         && version != "latest"
163:         && let Some(full_repo) = repo
164:     {
165:         // Try short name first (more common), e.g., "tectonic@0.15.0"
166:         if let Some(short_name) = full_repo.split('/').next_back() {
167:             candidates.push(format!("{}@{}", short_name, version));
168:         }
169:         // Also try full repo name, e.g., "tectonic-typesetting/tectonic@0.15.0"
170:         candidates.push(format!("{}@{}", full_repo, version));
171:     }
172: 
173:     for candidate in candidates {
174:         match resolver(candidate).await {
175:             Ok(res) => return Ok(res),
176:             Err(e) => {
177:                 if e.is_not_found() {
178:                     errors.push(e.into_eyre());
179:                 } else {
180:                     return Err(e.into_eyre());
181:                 }
182:             }
183:         }
184:     }
185:     Err(errors
186:         .pop()
187:         .unwrap_or_else(|| eyre::eyre!("No matching release found for {version}")))
188: }

190: /// Returns all possible aliases for the current platform (os, arch),
191: /// with the preferred spelling first (macos/x64, linux/x64, etc).
192: pub fn platform_aliases() -> Vec<(String, String)> {
193:     let os = std::env::consts::OS;
194:     let arch = std::env::consts::ARCH;
195:     let mut aliases = vec![];
196: 
197:     // OS aliases
198:     let os_aliases = match os {
199:         "macos" | "darwin" => vec!["macos", "darwin"],
200:         "linux" => vec!["linux"],
201:         "windows" => vec!["windows"],
202:         _ => vec![os],
203:     };
204: 
205:     // Arch aliases
206:     let arch_aliases = match arch {
207:         "x86_64" | "amd64" => vec!["x64", "amd64", "x86_64"],
208:         "aarch64" | "arm64" => vec!["arm64", "aarch64"],
209:         _ => vec![arch],
210:     };
211: 
212:     for os in &os_aliases {
213:         for arch in &arch_aliases {
214:             aliases.push((os.to_string(), arch.to_string()));
215:         }
216:     }
217:     aliases
218: }

220: /// Looks up a value in ToolVersionOptions using nested platform key format.
221: /// Supports nested format (platforms.macos-x64.url) with os-arch dash notation.
222: /// Also supports both "platforms" and "platform" prefixes.
223: pub fn lookup_platform_key(opts: &ToolVersionOptions, key_type: &str) -> Option<String> {
224:     // Try nested platform structure with os-arch format
225:     for (os, arch) in platform_aliases() {
226:         for prefix in ["platforms", "platform"] {
227:             // Try nested format: platforms.macos-x64.url
228:             let nested_key = format!("{prefix}.{os}-{arch}.{key_type}");
229:             if let Some(val) = opts.get_nested_string(&nested_key) {
230:                 return Some(val);
231:             }
232:             // Try flat format: platforms_macos_arm64_url
233:             let flat_key = format!("{prefix}_{os}_{arch}_{key_type}");
234:             if let Some(val) = opts.get_string(&flat_key) {
235:                 return Some(val);
236:             }
237:         }
238:     }
239:     None
240: }

242: /// Looks up an option value with platform-specific fallback.

245: /// # Arguments

247: /// * `key` - The option key to look up (e.g., "bin_path", "checksum")
248: ///

250: /// * `Some(value)` if found in platform-specific or base options
251: /// * `None` if not found
252: pub fn lookup_with_fallback(opts: &ToolVersionOptions, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
253:     lookup_platform_key(opts, key).or_else(|| opts.get_string(key))

310: /// Lists platform keys (e.g. "macos-x64") for which a given key_type exists (e.g. "url").
311: pub fn list_available_platforms_with_key(opts: &ToolVersionOptions, key_type: &str) -> Vec<String> {
312:     let mut set = IndexSet::new();
313: 
314:     // Gather from flat keys
315:     for (k, _) in opts.iter() {
316:         if let Some(rest) = k
317:             .strip_prefix("platforms_")
318:             .or_else(|| k.strip_prefix("platform_"))
319:             && let Some(platform_part) = rest.strip_suffix(&format!("_{}", key_type))
320:         {
321:             // Only convert the OS/arch separator underscore to a dash, preserving
322:             // underscores inside architecture names like x86_64
323:             let platform_key = if let Some((os_part, rest)) = platform_part.split_once('_') {
324:                 format!("{os_part}-{rest}")
325:             } else {
326:                 platform_part.to_string()
327:             };
328:             set.insert(platform_key);
329:         }
330:     }
331: 
332:     // Probe nested keys using shared patterns
333:     for os in OS_PATTERNS {
334:         for arch in ARCH_PATTERNS {
335:             for prefix in ["platforms", "platform"] {
336:                 let nested_key = format!("{prefix}.{os}-{arch}.{key_type}");
337:                 if opts.contains_key(&nested_key) {
338:                     set.insert(format!("{os}-{arch}"));
339:                 }
340:             }
341:         }
342:     }
343: 
344:     set.into_iter().collect()
345: }

347: pub fn template_string(template: &str, tv: &ToolVersion) -> String {
348:     // Check for legacy {version} syntax and emit deprecation warning
349:     if template.contains("{version}") && !template.contains("{{version}}") {
350:         deprecated_at!(
351:             "2026.3.0",
352:             "2027.3.0",
353:             "legacy-version-template",
354:             "Use {{{{ version }}}} instead of {{version}} in URL templates"
355:         );
356:         // Legacy support: replace {version} placeholder
357:         return template.replace("{version}", &tv.version);
358:     }
359: 
360:     if !crate::tera::contains_template_syntax(template) {
361:         return template.to_string();
362:     }
363: 
364:     // Use Tera rendering for templates
365:     // Supports {{ version }}, {{ os() }}, {{ arch() }}, etc.
366:     let mut ctx = crate::tera::BASE_CONTEXT.clone();
367:     ctx.insert("version", &tv.version);
368: 
369:     let mut tera = crate::tera::get_tera(None);
370:     match crate::tera::render_str(&mut tera, template, &ctx) {
371:         Ok(rendered) => rendered,
372:         Err(e) => {
373:             warn!("Failed to render template '{}': {}", template, e);
374:             template.to_string()
375:         }
376:     }
377: }

379: pub fn get_filename_from_url(url_str: &str) -> String {
380:     let filename = if let Ok(url) = url::Url::parse(url_str) {
381:         // Use proper URL parsing to get the path and extract filename
382:         url.path_segments()
383:             .and_then(|mut segments| segments.next_back())
384:             .map(|s| s.to_string())
385:             .unwrap_or_else(|| url_str.to_string())
386:     } else {
387:         // Fallback to simple parsing for non-URL strings or malformed URLs
388:         url_str
389:             .split('/')
390:             .next_back()
391:             .unwrap_or(url_str)
392:             .to_string()
393:     };
394:     urlencoding::decode(&filename)
395:         .map(|s| s.to_string())
396:         .unwrap_or(filename)
397: }

399: pub fn install_artifact(
400:     tv: &crate::toolset::ToolVersion,
401:     file_path: &Path,
402:     opts: &ToolVersionOptions,
403:     pr: Option<&dyn SingleReport>,
404: ) -> eyre::Result<()> {
405:     let install_path = tv.install_path();
406:     let mut strip_components = lookup_platform_key(opts, "strip_components")
407:         .or_else(|| opts.get_string("strip_components"))
408:         .and_then(|s| s.parse().ok());
409: 
410:     file::remove_all(&install_path)?;
411:     file::create_dir_all(&install_path)?;
412: 
413:     // Use TarFormat for format detection
414:     // Check for explicit format option first, then fall back to file extension
415:     let format = if let Some(format_opt) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "format") {
416:         file::TarFormat::from_ext(&format_opt)
417:     } else {
418:         file::TarFormat::from_file_name(
419:             &file_path.file_name().unwrap_or_default().to_string_lossy(),
420:         )
421:     };
422: 
423:     // Get file extension and detect format
424:     let file_name = file_path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
425: 
426:     if !format.is_archive() && format != file::TarFormat::Raw {
427:         // Handle compressed single binary
428:         let ext = Path::new(&*file_name)
429:             .extension()
430:             .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
431:             .unwrap_or("")
432:             .to_string();
433:         let decompressed_name = file_name.trim_end_matches(&format!(".{}", ext));
434: 
435:         // Determine the destination path with support for bin_path
436:         let dest = if let Some(bin_path_template) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin_path") {
437:             let bin_path = template_string(&bin_path_template, tv);
438:             let bin_dir = install_path.join(&bin_path);
439:             file::create_dir_all(&bin_dir)?;
440:             bin_dir.join(decompressed_name)
441:         } else if let Some(bin_name) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin") {
442:             install_path.join(&bin_name)
443:         } else {
444:             // Auto-clean binary names by removing OS/arch suffixes
445:             let cleaned_name = clean_binary_name(decompressed_name, Some(&tv.ba().tool_name));
446:             install_path.join(cleaned_name)
447:         };
448: 
449:         file::untar(
450:             file_path,
451:             &dest,
452:             &file::TarOptions {
453:                 pr,
454:                 ..file::TarOptions::new(format)
455:             },
456:         )?;
457: 
458:         file::make_executable(&dest)?;
459:     } else if format == file::TarFormat::Raw {
460:         // Copy the file directly to the bin_path directory or install_path
461:         if let Some(bin_path_template) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin_path") {
462:             let bin_path = template_string(&bin_path_template, tv);
463:             let bin_dir = install_path.join(&bin_path);
464:             file::create_dir_all(&bin_dir)?;
465:             let dest = bin_dir.join(file_path.file_name().unwrap());
466:             file::copy(file_path, &dest)?;
467:             file::make_executable(&dest)?;
468:         } else if let Some(bin_name) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin") {
469:             // If bin is specified, rename the file to this name
470:             let dest = install_path.join(&bin_name);
471:             file::copy(file_path, &dest)?;
472:             file::make_executable(&dest)?;
473:         } else {
474:             // Always auto-clean binary names by removing OS/arch suffixes
475:             let original_name = file_path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
476:             let cleaned_name = clean_binary_name(&original_name, Some(&tv.ba().tool_name));
477:             let dest = install_path.join(cleaned_name);
478:             file::copy(file_path, &dest)?;
479:             file::make_executable(&dest)?;
480:         }
481:     } else {
482:         // Handle archive formats
483:         // Auto-detect if we need strip_components=1 before extracting
484:         // Only do this if strip_components was not explicitly set by the user AND bin_path is not configured
485:         if strip_components.is_none()
486:             && lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin_path").is_none()
487:             && let Ok(should_strip) = file::should_strip_components(file_path, format)
488:             && should_strip
489:         {
490:             debug!("Auto-detected single directory archive, extracting with strip_components=1");
491:             strip_components = Some(1);
492:         }
493:         let tar_opts = file::TarOptions {
494:             strip_components: strip_components.unwrap_or(0),
495:             pr,
496:             ..file::TarOptions::new(format)
497:         };
498: 
499:         // Extract with determined strip_components
500:         file::untar(file_path, &install_path, &tar_opts)?;
501: 
502:         // Extract just the repo name from tool_name (e.g., "opsgenie/opsgenie-lamp" -> "opsgenie-lamp")
503:         let full_tool_name = tv.ba().tool_name.as_str();
504:         let tool_name = full_tool_name.rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or(full_tool_name);
505: 
506:         // Determine search directory based on bin_path option
507:         let explicit_bin_path = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin_path")
508:             .map(|t| install_path.join(template_string(&t, tv)));
509: 
510:         // Handle bin= option for archives (renames executable to specified name)
511:         // bin= values are relative to install_path, so always use install_path or explicit bin_path
512:         if let Some(bin_name) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin") {
513:             let search_dir = explicit_bin_path.as_deref().unwrap_or(&install_path);
514:             make_configured_bin_executable(search_dir, &bin_name)?;
515:             rename_executable_in_dir(search_dir, &bin_name, Some(tool_name))?;
516:         }
517: 
518:         // Handle rename_exe option for archives
519:         // When bin_path is not explicitly set, auto-detect bin/ subdirectory to match
520:         // the same logic used by discover_bin_paths() for PATH construction
521:         if let Some(rename_to) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "rename_exe") {
522:             let search_dir = if let Some(ref dir) = explicit_bin_path {
523:                 dir.clone()
524:             } else {
525:                 let bin_dir = install_path.join("bin");
526:                 if bin_dir.is_dir() {
527:                     bin_dir
528:                 } else {
529:                     install_path.clone()
530:                 }
531:             };
532:             rename_executable_in_dir(&search_dir, &rename_to, Some(tool_name))?;
533:         }
534:     }
535:     Ok(())
536: }

538: fn make_configured_bin_executable(search_dir: &Path, bin_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
539:     let bin_path = search_dir.join(bin_name);
540:     if bin_path.is_file() {
541:         file::make_executable(bin_path)?;
542:     }
543:     Ok(())

546: pub fn verify_artifact(
547:     _tv: &crate::toolset::ToolVersion,
548:     file_path: &Path,
549:     opts: &crate::toolset::ToolVersionOptions,
550:     pr: Option<&dyn SingleReport>,
551: ) -> Result<()> {
552:     // Check platform-specific checksum first, then fall back to generic
553:     let checksum = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "checksum");
554: 
555:     if let Some(checksum) = checksum {
556:         verify_checksum_str(file_path, &checksum, pr)?;
557:     }
558: 
559:     // Check platform-specific size first, then fall back to generic
560:     let size_str = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "size");
561: 
562:     if let Some(size_str) = size_str {
563:         let expected_size: u64 = size_str.parse()?;
564:         let actual_size = file_path.metadata()?.len();
565:         if actual_size != expected_size {
566:             bail!(
567:                 "Size mismatch: expected {}, got {}",
568:                 expected_size,
569:                 actual_size
570:             );
571:         }
572:     }
573: 
574:     Ok(())
575: }
576: 
577: pub fn verify_checksum_str(
578:     file_path: &Path,
579:     checksum: &str,
580:     pr: Option<&dyn SingleReport>,
581: ) -> Result<()> {
582:     if let Some((algo, hash_str)) = checksum.split_once(':') {
583:         hash::ensure_checksum(file_path, hash_str, pr, algo)?;
584:     } else {
585:         bail!("Invalid checksum format: {}", checksum);
586:     }
587:     Ok(())
588: }

590: /// File extensions that indicate non-binary files.
591: const SKIP_EXTENSIONS: &[&str] = &[".txt", ".md", ".json", ".yml", ".yaml"];
592: 
593: /// File names (case-insensitive) that should be skipped when looking for executables.
594: const SKIP_FILE_NAMES: &[&str] = &["LICENSE", "README"];
595: 
596: /// Checks if a file should be skipped when searching for executables.
597: ///
598: /// # Arguments
599: /// * `file_name` - The file name to check
600: /// * `strict` - If true, also checks against SKIP_FILE_NAMES and README.* patterns
601: ///
602: /// # Returns
603: /// * `true` if the file should be skipped (not a binary)
604: /// * `false` if the file might be a binary
605: fn should_skip_file(file_name: &str, strict: bool) -> bool {
606:     // Skip hidden files
607:     if file_name.starts_with('.') {
608:         return true;
609:     }
610: 
611:     // Skip known non-binary extensions
612:     if SKIP_EXTENSIONS.iter().any(|ext| file_name.ends_with(ext)) {
613:         return true;
614:     }
615: 
616:     // In strict mode, also skip LICENSE/README files
617:     if strict {
618:         let upper = file_name.to_uppercase();
619:         if SKIP_FILE_NAMES.iter().any(|name| upper == *name) || upper.starts_with("README.") {
620:             return true;
621:         }
622:     }
623: 
624:     false
625: }
626: 
627: /// Renames the first executable file found in a directory to a new name.
628: /// Used by the `rename_exe` and `bin` options to rename binaries after archive extraction.
629: ///
630: /// # Parameters
631: /// - `dir`: The directory to search for executables
632: /// - `new_name`: The new name for the executable
633: /// - `tool_name`: Optional hint for finding non-executable files by name matching.
634: ///   When provided, if no executable is found, will search for files matching the tool name
635: ///   and make them executable before renaming.
636: pub fn rename_executable_in_dir(
637:     dir: &Path,
638:     new_name: &str,
639:     tool_name: Option<&str>,
640: ) -> eyre::Result<()> {
641:     let target_path = dir.join(new_name);
642: 
643:     // Check if target already exists before iterating
644:     // (read_dir order is non-deterministic, so we must check first)
645:     if target_path.is_file() && file::is_executable(&target_path) {
646:         return Ok(());
647:     }
648: 
649:     // Check for stripped macOS .app bundle (Contents/MacOS at root level)
650:     // This happens when auto-strip removes the .app wrapper directory
651:     let contents_macos = dir.join("Contents").join("MacOS");
652:     if contents_macos.is_dir() {
653:         // Rename within Contents/MacOS instead of moving to root
654:         let target_in_macos = contents_macos.join(new_name);
655:         if rename_executable_in_app_bundle(&contents_macos, &target_in_macos, tool_name)? {
656:             return Ok(());
657:         }
658:     }
659: 
660:     // Check for macOS .app bundles and look inside Contents/MacOS/
661:     for entry in file::ls(dir)? {
662:         if entry.is_dir() {
663:             let dir_name = entry.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
664:             if dir_name.ends_with(".app") {
665:                 let macos_dir = entry.join("Contents").join("MacOS");
666:                 if macos_dir.is_dir() {
667:                     // Try to rename executable inside the .app bundle
668:                     if rename_executable_in_app_bundle(&macos_dir, &target_path, tool_name)? {
669:                         return Ok(());
670:                     }
671:                 }
672:             }
673:         }
674:     }
675: 
676:     // First pass: Find executables in the directory (non-recursive for top level)
677:     // When tool_name is available, prefer an exact match, then a substring match,
678:     // then fall back to the first executable found.
679:     let mut substring_match: Option<PathBuf> = None;
680:     let mut fallback_path: Option<PathBuf> = None;
681:     for path in file::ls(dir)? {
682:         if path.is_file() && file::is_executable(&path) {
683:             let file_name = path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
684:             if should_skip_file(&file_name, false) {
685:                 continue;
686:             }
687:             if let Some(tool_name) = tool_name {
688:                 if *file_name == *tool_name {
689:                     let target_path_with_extension =
690:                         keep_required_extensions(dir, &file_name, new_name, target_path);
691:                     file::rename(&path, &target_path_with_extension)?;
692:                     debug!(
693:                         "Renamed {} to {}",
694:                         path.display(),
695:                         target_path_with_extension.display()
696:                     );
697:                     return Ok(());
698:                 }
699:                 if file_name.contains(tool_name) {
700:                     if substring_match.is_none() {
701:                         substring_match = Some(path);
702:                     }
703:                 } else if fallback_path.is_none() {
704:                     fallback_path = Some(path);
705:                 }
706:             } else {
707:                 let target_path_with_extension =
708:                     keep_required_extensions(dir, &file_name, new_name, target_path);
709:                 file::rename(&path, &target_path_with_extension)?;
710:                 debug!(
711:                     "Renamed {} to {}",
712:                     path.display(),
713:                     target_path_with_extension.display()
714:                 );
715:                 return Ok(());
716:             }
717:         }
718:     }
719:     // Prefer substring match over arbitrary fallback
720:     let best_match = substring_match.or(fallback_path);
721:     if let Some(path) = best_match {
722:         let file_name = path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
723:         let target_path_with_extension =
724:             keep_required_extensions(dir, &file_name, new_name, target_path.clone());
725:         file::rename(&path, &target_path_with_extension)?;
726:         debug!(
727:             "Renamed {} to {} (fallback)",
728:             path.display(),
729:             target_path_with_extension.display()
730:         );
731:         return Ok(());
732:     }
733: 
734:     // Second pass: Find non-executable files by name matching (for ZIP archives without exec bit)
735:     if let Some(tool_name) = tool_name {
736:         for path in file::ls(dir)? {
737:             if path.is_file() {
738:                 let file_name = path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
739:                 if should_skip_file(&file_name, true) {
740:                     continue;
741:                 }
742: 
743:                 // Check if filename matches tool name pattern or the target name
744:                 if file_name.contains(tool_name) || *file_name == *new_name {
745:                     let target_path_with_extension =
746:                         keep_required_extensions(dir, &file_name, new_name, target_path);
747: 
748:                     file::make_executable(&path)?;
749:                     file::rename(&path, &target_path_with_extension)?;
750:                     debug!(
751:                         "Found and renamed {} to {} (added exec permissions)",
752:                         path.display(),
753:                         target_path_with_extension.display()
754:                     );
755:                     return Ok(());
756:                 }
757:             }
758:         }
759:     }
760: 
761:     Ok(())
762: }
763: 
764: /// Helper function to rename executable inside a macOS .app bundle
765: fn rename_executable_in_app_bundle(
766:     macos_dir: &Path,
767:     target_path: &Path,
768:     tool_name: Option<&str>,
769: ) -> eyre::Result<bool> {
770:     // Find the first executable in the Contents/MacOS directory
771:     for path in file::ls(macos_dir)? {
772:         if path.is_file() && file::is_executable(&path) {
773:             let file_name = path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
774:             if should_skip_file(&file_name, false) {
775:                 continue;
776:             }
777:             file::rename(&path, target_path)?;
778:             debug!(
779:                 "Renamed .app bundle executable {} to {}",
780:                 path.display(),
781:                 target_path.display()
782:             );
783:             return Ok(true);
784:         }
785:     }
786: 
787:     // If no executable found, try matching by tool name
788:     if let Some(tool_name) = tool_name {
789:         for path in file::ls(macos_dir)? {
790:             if path.is_file() {
791:                 let file_name = path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
792:                 if should_skip_file(&file_name, true) {
793:                     continue;
794:                 }
795:                 if file_name.to_lowercase().contains(&tool_name.to_lowercase()) {
796:                     file::make_executable(&path)?;
797:                     file::rename(&path, target_path)?;
798:                     debug!(
799:                         "Found and renamed .app bundle file {} to {} (added exec permissions)",
800:                         path.display(),
801:                         target_path.display()
802:                     );
803:                     return Ok(true);
804:                 }
805:             }
806:         }
807:     }
808: 
809:     Ok(false)
810: }

812: fn keep_required_extensions(
813:     dir: &Path,
814:     file_name: &str,
815:     new_name: &str,
816:     target_path: PathBuf,
817: ) -> PathBuf {
818:     if cfg!(windows) {
819:         return keep_extensions(
820:             dir,
821:             file_name,
822:             new_name,
823:             target_path,
824:             &[".exe", ".cmd", ".bat"],
825:         );
826:     }
827:     target_path
828: }
829: 
830: fn keep_extensions(
831:     dir: &Path,
832:     file_name: &str,
833:     new_name: &str,
834:     target_path: PathBuf,
835:     exts: &[&str],
836: ) -> PathBuf {
837:     for ext in exts {
838:         if file_name.to_lowercase().ends_with(ext) && !new_name.to_lowercase().ends_with(ext) {
839:             return dir.join(format!("{}{}", new_name, ext));
840:         }
841:     }
842:     target_path
843: }
